revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: '{{日本の古墳 |名称=鳥居前古墳 |画像= |所在地=京都府乙訓郡大山崎町円明寺 |位置= |形状=帆立貝形古墳 |規模=墳丘...'
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称=鳥居前古墳 |画像= |所在地=京都府乙訓郡大山崎町円明寺 |位置= |形状=帆立貝形古墳 |規模=墳丘長51ｍ |築造年代= 4世紀末頃から5世紀初頭 |埋葬施設=竪穴式石室 |被葬者= |出土品=画文帯神獣鏡片 勾玉 巴形銅器 |史跡指定= |特記事項= }} '''鳥居前古墳'''（とりいまえこふん）は[[京都府]][[乙訓郡]][[大山崎町]]に所在する[[帆立貝形古墳]]。1967年に分布調査により発見され、京都府と[[大阪大学]]により前後3回発掘調査が行なわれている。 == 概要== 鳥居前古墳は、天下分け目の戦いで有名な[[天王山]]の北側の麓にある。1967年に京都府教育委員会による分布調査により存在が明らかになり、1969年に後円部の[[竪穴式石室]]と墳丘の1部が発掘調査されている。当初は、全長70メートルの[[前方後円墳]]と推定されたが、その後の調査により、帆立貝形古墳に訂正されている。 ===埋葬主体と副葬品=== 後円部に竪穴式石室が1基あり、石室の主軸は墳丘主軸に斜交する。規模は、墓壙が9．0×4.4メートル。石室が長さ6.5メートル、西端幅1.2メートル、東端幅0.77メートルを測る。石室の上部の石と天井石は既に失われていた。石室内には長さ5.3メートルの[[割竹形木棺]]があったと推定される。粘土棺床内からは[[勾玉]]2、[[管玉]]23、鉄刀8、短剣21、短刀9、巴形銅器2が出土している。短剣、短刀は鞘、茎などの木質部が良く残っていた。それ以外の石室床面から鉄剣1、鉄鏃7、巴形銅器6、短甲片、小札3、刀子3が、また石室内の攪乱土から画文帯帯神獣鏡などの破片3、勾玉1、管玉5、刀剣・鉄鏃・短甲・鉄斧・刀子などの破片が出土している。 ===墳丘=== 墳丘全体は竹林であったので著しく改変されており、測量調査などにより前方後円墳と推定されたものの、その後の大阪大学による墳丘の発掘調査により帆立貝形古墳と判断されている。3段に築成され、規模は全長51メートル、後円部径38.4メートル、前方部長12.5メートル、前方部幅推定31メートル、後円部高さ6.5メートル以上と推定されている。墳丘表面には葺石を置いていた形跡があるが原位置を保つものは少なかった。古墳から500メートル程度内の河床や崖から採取されたものと推定される墳丘の各段には[[円筒埴輪]]列をめぐらしていた形跡があり、円筒埴輪以外には壷形埴輪や蓋形埴輪が出土している。なかでも蓋形埴輪は前後3回の調査で7個体出土している。 ===築造時期=== 以上などのことから、[[都出比呂志]]は当古墳の築造時期を西暦400年前後と推定している
==参考文献== 都出比呂志他 『鳥居前古墳 -総括編‐』 大阪大学考古学研究室
==外部リンク==
*[http://www.isekiwalker.com/iseki/108736/] 鳥居前古墳 遺跡ウォーカーβ
{{デフォルトソート:とりいまえこふん}}

{{日本の古墳 |名称=鳥居前古墳 |画像= |所在地=京都府乙訓郡大山崎町円明寺 |位置= |形状=帆立貝形古墳 |規模=墳丘長51ｍ |築造年代= 4世紀末頃から5世紀初頭 |埋葬施設=竪穴式石室 |被葬者= |出土品=画文帯神獣鏡片 勾玉 巴形銅器 |史跡指定= |特記事項= }} '''鳥居前古墳'''（とりいまえこふん）は[[京都府]][[乙訓郡]][[大山崎町]]に所在する[[帆立貝形古墳]]。1967年に分布調査により発見され、京都府と[[大阪大学]]により前後3回発掘調査が行なわれている。 == 概要== 鳥居前古墳は、天下分け目の戦いで有名な[[天王山]]の北側の麓にある。1967年に京都府教育委員会による分布調査により存在が明らかになり、1969年に後円部の[[竪穴式石室]]と墳丘の1部が発掘調査されている。当初は、全長70メートルの[[前方後円墳]]と推定されたが、その後の調査により、帆立貝形古墳に訂正されている。 ===埋葬主体と副葬品=== 後円部に竪穴式石室が1基あり、石室の主軸は墳丘主軸に斜交する。規模は、墓壙が9．0×4.4メートル。石室が長さ6.5メートル、西端幅1.2メートル、東端幅0.77メートルを測る。石室の上部の石と天井石は既に失われていた。石室内には長さ5.3メートルの[[割竹形木棺]]があったと推定される。粘土棺床内からは[[勾玉]]2、[[管玉]]23、鉄刀8、短剣21、短刀9、巴形銅器2が出土している。短剣、短刀は鞘、茎などの木質部が良く残っていた。それ以外の石室床面から鉄剣1、鉄鏃7、巴形銅器6、短甲片、小札3、刀子3が、また石室内の攪乱土から画文帯帯神獣鏡などの破片3、勾玉1、管玉5、刀剣・鉄鏃・短甲・鉄斧・刀子などの破片が出土している。 ===墳丘=== 墳丘全体は竹林であったので著しく改変されており、測量調査などにより前方後円墳と推定されたものの、その後の大阪大学による墳丘の発掘調査により帆立貝形古墳と判断されている。3段に築成され、規模は全長51メートル、後円部径38.4メートル、前方部長12.5メートル、前方部幅推定31メートル、後円部高さ6.5メートル以上と推定されている。墳丘表面には葺石を置いていた形跡があるが原位置を保つものは少なかった。古墳から500メートル程度内の河床や崖から採取されたものと推定される墳丘の各段には[[円筒埴輪]]列をめぐらしていた形跡があり、円筒埴輪以外には壷形埴輪や蓋形埴輪が出土している。なかでも蓋形埴輪は前後3回の調査で7個体出土している。 ===築造時期=== 以上などのことから、[[都出比呂志]]は当古墳の築造時期を西暦400年前後と推定している。
==参考文献== 都出比呂志他 『鳥居前古墳 -総括編-』 大阪大学考古学研究室 1990年
==外部リンク== *[http://www.isekiwalker.com/iseki/108736/ 鳥居前古墳] 遺跡ウォーカーβ
{{デフォルトソート:とりいまえこふん}} [[category:古墳]] [[category:京都府の古墳]] [[category:大山崎町]] [[category:山城国]]

revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称=鳥居前古墳 |画像= |所在地=京都府乙訓郡大山崎町円明寺 |位置= |形状=帆立貝形古墳 |規模=墳丘長51ｍ　 |築造年代= 4世紀末頃から5世紀初頭 |埋葬施設=竪穴式石室 |被葬者= |出土品=画文帯神獣鏡片　勾玉　巴形銅器 |史跡指定=国の[[史跡]]「鳥居前古墳」
（「乙訓古墳群」に包含） |特記事項= }} '''鳥居前古墳'''（とりいまえこふん）は[[京都府]][[乙訓郡]][[大山崎町]]に所在する[[帆立貝形古墳]]。国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「乙訓古墳群」のうち）。 1967年に分布調査により発見され、京都府と[[大阪大学]]により前後3回発掘調査が行なわれている。 == 概要== 鳥居前古墳は、天下分け目の戦いで有名な[[天王山]]の北側の麓、標高97メートルの丘陵上にある。1967年に京都府教育委員会による分布調査により存在が明らかになり、1969年に後円部の[[竪穴式石室]]と墳丘の1部が発掘調査されている。当初は、全長70メートルの[[前方後円墳]]と推定されたが、その後の調査により、帆立貝形古墳に訂正されている。 ===埋葬主体と副葬品=== 後円部に竪穴式石室が1基あり、石室の主軸は墳丘主軸に斜交する。規模は、墓壙が9．0×4.4メートル。石室は長さ6.5メートル、西端幅1.2メートル、東端幅0.77メートルを測る。石室の上部の石と天井石は既に失われていた。石室内には長さ5.3メートルの[[割竹形木棺]]があったと推定される。木棺を置いた粘土棺床内からは[[勾玉]]2、[[管玉]]23、鉄刀8、短剣21、短刀9、巴形銅器2が出土している。短剣、短刀は鞘、茎などの木質部が良く残っていた。それ以外の石室床面から鉄剣1、鉄鏃7、巴形銅器6、短甲片、小札3、刀子3が、また石室内の攪乱土から画文帯帯神獣鏡などの破片3、勾玉1、管玉5、刀剣・鉄鏃・短甲・鉄斧・刀子などの破片が出土している。 ===墳丘=== 墳丘全体は竹林であったので著しく改変されており、測量調査などにより前方後円墳と推定されたものの、その後の大阪大学による墳丘の発掘調査によって、帆立貝形古墳と判断されている。墳丘は3段に築成されており、規模は全長51メートル、後円部径38.4メートル、前方部長12.5メートル、前方部幅推定31メートル、後円部高さ6.5メートル以上と推定される。墳丘表面には葺石で覆っていた形跡があるが、原位置を保つものは少なかった。葺石の石材は古墳から500メートル程度内の河床や崖から採取されたものと推定される。墳丘の各段には[[円筒埴輪]]列をめぐらしていた形跡があり、円筒埴輪以外には壷形埴輪や蓋形埴輪が出土している。なかでも蓋形埴輪は前後3回の調査で7個体出土している。 ===築造時期=== 以上などのことから、[[都出比呂志]]は当古墳の築造時期を西暦400年前後と推定している。 == 文化財 == === 国の史跡 === * 鳥居前古墳（史跡「乙訓古墳群」のうち） *: 平成28年3月1日、既指定の史跡「天皇の杜古墳」・「[[恵解山古墳]]」・「[[寺戸大塚古墳]]」の3ヶ所に鳥居前古墳など8基を追加指定、「乙訓古墳群」に名称変更平成28年3月1日文部科学省告示第31号。。 ==参考文献== 都出比呂志他　『鳥居前古墳　-総括編-』　大阪大学考古学研究室　1990年 ==外部リンク== *[http://www.isekiwalker.com/iseki/108736/ 鳥居前古墳]　遺跡ウォーカーβ {{デフォルトソート:とりいまえこふん}} [[category:京都府の古墳]] [[category:大山崎町の歴史]] [[Category:帆立貝形古墳]] [[Category:京都府にある国指定の史跡]]

{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|54|42.5|N|135|40|39.2|E}}}} |特記事項= }} '''鳥居前古墳'''（とりいまえこふん）は[[京都府]][[乙訓郡]][[大山崎町]]に所在する[[帆立貝形古墳]]。国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「乙訓古墳群」のうち）。1967年（昭和42年）に分布調査により発見され、京都府と[[大阪大学]]により前後3回の[[発掘調査]]が行なわれている。 == 概要 == 鳥居前古墳は、天下分け目の戦いで有名な[[天王山]]の北側の麓、標高97メートルの丘陵上にある。1967年（昭和42年）に京都府教育委員会による分布調査により存在が明らかになり、1969年（昭和44年）に後円部の[[竪穴式石室]]と墳丘の一部が発掘調査されている。当初は、全長70メートルの[[前方後円墳]]と推定されたが、その後の調査により、帆立貝形古墳に訂正されている。 === 埋葬主体と副葬品 === 後円部に竪穴式石室が1基あり、石室の主軸は墳丘主軸に斜交する。規模は、墓壙が9.0×4.4メートル。石室は長さ6.5メートル、西端幅1.2メートル、東端幅0.77メートルを測る。石室の上部の石と天井石は既に失われていた。石室内には長さ5.3メートルの[[割竹形木棺]]があったと推定される。木棺を置いた粘土棺床内からは[[勾玉]]2、[[管玉]]23、[[直刀|鉄刀]]8、[[短剣]]21、短刀9、巴形銅器2が出土している。短剣、短刀は鞘、茎などの木質部が良く残っていた。それ以外の石室床面から鉄剣1、[[鉄鏃]]7、巴形銅器6、[[短甲]]片、[[挂甲|小札]]3、[[刀子]]3が、また石室内の攪乱土から画文帯帯神獣鏡などの破片3、勾玉1、管玉5、刀剣・鉄鏃・短甲・鉄斧・刀子などの破片が出土している。 === 墳丘 === 墳丘全体は竹林であったので著しく改変されており、測量調査などにより前方後円墳と推定されたものの、その後の大阪大学による墳丘の発掘調査によって、帆立貝形古墳と判断されている。墳丘は3段に築成されており、規模は全長51メートル、後円部径38.4メートル、前方部長12.5メートル、前方部幅推定31メートル、後円部高さ6.5メートル以上と推定される。墳丘表面には葺石で覆っていた形跡があるが、原位置を保つものは少なかった。葺石の石材は古墳から500メートル程度内の河床や崖から採取されたものと推定される。墳丘の各段には[[円筒埴輪]]列をめぐらしていた形跡があり、円筒埴輪以外には壷形埴輪や蓋形埴輪が出土している。なかでも蓋形埴輪は前後3回の調査で7個体出土している。 === 築造時期 === 以上などのことから、[[都出比呂志]]は当古墳の築造時期を西暦400年前後と推定している。 == 文化財 == === 国の史跡 === * 鳥居前古墳（史跡「乙訓古墳群」のうち） *: 2016年（平成28年）3月1日、既指定の史跡「[[天皇の杜古墳]]」・「[[恵解山古墳]]」・「[[寺戸大塚古墳]]」の3ヶ所に鳥居前古墳など8基を追加指定、「乙訓古墳群」に名称変更平成28年（2016年）3月1日文部科学省告示第31号。。 ==脚注== {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == *{{Cite book|和書|last=都出|first=比呂志|authorlink=都出比呂志|title=鳥居前古墳-総括編-|publisher=大阪大学考古学研究室|year=1990|date=1990|ref=harv}} *{{Cite book|和書|author=大山崎町教育委員会|title=史跡 乙訓古墳群 鳥居前古墳|publisher=大山崎町|url=http://www.town.oyamazaki.kyoto.jp/material/files/group/51/ri-fu.pdf|year=2019|date=2019-06-27|ref=harv}} == 外部リンク == *[http://www.isekiwalker.com/iseki/108736/ 鳥居前古墳]　遺跡ウォーカーβ {{デフォルトソート:とりいまえこふん}} [[category:京都府の古墳]] [[category:大山崎町の歴史]] [[Category:帆立貝形古墳]] [[Category:京都府にある国指定の史跡]]

鳥居前古墳は、天下分け目の戦いで有名な[[天王山]]の北側の麓、標高97メートルの丘陵上にある。1967年（昭和42年）に京都府教育委員会による分布調査により存在が明らかになり、1969年（昭和44年）に後円部の[[竪穴式石室]]と墳丘の一部が発掘調査されている。当初は、全長70メートルの[[前方後円墳]]と推定されたが、その後の調査により、帆立貝形古墳に訂正されている。 === 埋葬主体と副葬品 === 後円部に竪穴式石室が1基あり、石室の主軸は墳丘主軸に斜交する。規模は、墓壙が9.0×4.4メートル。石室は長さ6.5メートル、西端幅1.2メートル、東端幅0.77メートルを測る。石室の上部の石と天井石は既に失われていた。石室内には長さ5.3メートルの[[割竹形木棺]]があったと推定される。木棺を置いた粘土棺床内からは[[勾玉]]2、[[管玉]]23、[[直刀|鉄刀]]8、[[短剣]]21、短刀9、巴形銅器2が出土している。短剣、短刀は鞘、茎などの木質部が良く残っていた。それ以外の石室床面から鉄剣1、[[鉄鏃]]7、巴形銅器6、[[短甲]]片、[[挂甲|小札]]3、[[刀子]]3が、また石室内の攪乱土から画文帯帯神獣鏡などの破片3、勾玉1、管玉5、刀剣・鉄鏃・短甲・鉄斧・刀子などの破片が出土している。 === 墳丘 === 墳丘全体は竹林であったので著しく改変されており、測量調査などにより前方後円墳と推定されたものの、その後の大阪大学による墳丘の発掘調査によって、帆立貝形古墳と判断されている。墳丘は3段に築成されており、規模は全長51メートル、後円部径38.4メートル、前方部長12.5メートル、前方部幅推定31メートル、後円部高さ6.5メートル以上と推定される。墳丘表面には葺石で覆っていた形跡があるが、原位置を保つものは少なかった。葺石の石材は古墳から500メートル程度内の河床や崖から採取されたものと推定される。墳丘の各段には[[円筒埴輪]]列をめぐらしていた形跡があり、円筒埴輪以外には壷形埴輪や蓋形埴輪が出土している。なかでも蓋形埴輪は前後3回の調査で7個体出土している。 === 築造時期 === 以上などのことから、[[都出比呂志]]は当古墳の築造時期を西暦400年前後と推定している。 == 文化財 == === 国の史跡 === * 鳥居前古墳（史跡「乙訓古墳群」のうち） *: 2016年（平成28年）3月1日、既指定の史跡「[[天皇の杜古墳]]」・「[[恵解山古墳]]」・「[[寺戸大塚古墳]]」の3ヶ所に鳥居前古墳など8基を追加指定、「乙訓古墳群」に名称変更平成28年（2016年）3月1日文部科学省告示第31号。。 ==脚注== {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == *{{Cite book|和書|last=都出|first=比呂志|authorlink=都出比呂志|title=鳥居前古墳-総括編-|publisher=大阪大学考古学研究室|year=1990|date=1990|ref=harv}} *{{Cite book|和書|author=大山崎町教育委員会|title=史跡 乙訓古墳群 鳥居前古墳|publisher=大山崎町|url=http://www.town.oyamazaki.kyoto.jp/material/files/group/51/ri-fu.pdf|year=2019|date=2019-06-27|ref=harv}} == 外部リンク == *[http://www.isekiwalker.com/iseki/108736/ 鳥居前古墳]　遺跡ウォーカーβ {{デフォルトソート:とりいまえこふん}} [[Category:乙訓古墳群]] [[category:京都府の古墳]] [[category:大山崎町の歴史]] [[Category:帆立貝形古墳]] [[Category:京都府にある国指定の史跡]]

'''鳥居前古墳'''（とりいまえこふん）は[[京都府]][[乙訓郡]][[大山崎町]]に所在する[[帆立貝形古墳]]。国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「乙訓古墳群」のうち）。1967年（昭和42年）に分布調査により発見され、京都府と[[大阪大学]]により前後3回の[[発掘調査]]が行なわれている。 == 概要 == 鳥居前古墳は、天下分け目の戦いで有名な[[天王山]]の北側の麓、標高97メートルの丘陵上にある。1967年（昭和42年）に京都府教育委員会による分布調査により存在が明らかになり、1969年（昭和44年）に後円部の[[竪穴式石室]]と墳丘の一部が発掘調査されている。当初は、全長70メートルの[[前方後円墳]]と推定されたが、その後の調査により、帆立貝形古墳に訂正されている。 === 埋葬主体と副葬品 墳丘全体は竹林であったので著しく改変されており、測量調査などにより前方後円墳と推定されたものの、その後の大阪大学による墳丘の発掘調査によって、帆立貝形古墳と判断されている。墳丘は3段に築成されており、規模は全長51メートル、後円部径38.4メートル、前方部長12.5メートル、前方部幅推定31メートル、後円部高さ6.5メートル以上と推定される。墳丘表面には葺石で覆っていた形跡があるが、原位置を保つものは少なかった。葺石の石材は古墳から500メートル程度内の河床や崖から採取されたものと推定される。墳丘の各段には[[円筒埴輪]]列をめぐらしていた形跡があり、円筒埴輪以外には壷形埴輪や蓋形埴輪が出土している。なかでも蓋形埴輪は前後3回の調査で7個体出土している。 === 築造時期 === 以上などのことから、[[都出比呂志]]は当古墳の築造時期を西暦400年前後と推定している。 == 文化財 == === 国の史跡 === * 鳥居前古墳（史跡「乙訓古墳群」のうち） *: '''Toriimae Kofun''' (鳥居前古墳) is a scallop-shaped burial mound (帆立貝形古墳) located in {{ill|Ōyamazaki Town|ja|大山崎町}}, Otokuni District, Kyoto Prefecture. It is designated as a National Historic Site as part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Group|ja|乙訓古墳群}}. The mound was discovered in a 1967 survey and has since undergone three archaeological investigations by Kyoto Prefecture and Osaka University. == Overview == Toriimae Kofun is situated on the northern slopes of Mount Tennozan, known for the famous Battle of Yamazaki, at an elevation of 97 meters. The burial mound was identified during a distribution survey conducted by the Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education in 1967. Excavations of the rear circular section and part of the mound were conducted in 1969. Initially thought to be a keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳), subsequent investigations revised the designation to a scallop-shaped mound. === Burial Facility and Grave Goods === The rear circular section contains a single vertical stone chamber (竪穴式石室), oriented diagonally to the main axis of the mound. Measurements are as follows: * Burial chamber: 9.0 × 4.4 meters * Stone chamber: Length 6.5 meters, width 1.2 meters (west end), 0.77 meters (east end) The chamber's upper stones and ceiling stones were already missing. A clay bed within the chamber likely housed a split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin (割竹形木棺) of 5.3 meters in length. The coffin bed contained: * 2 magatama * 23 cylindrical beads (管玉) * 8 iron swords * 21 daggers * 9 short swords * 2 comma-shaped copper objects (巴形銅器) Additional grave goods, including swords, arrows, armor fragments, and fragments of a deity-beast mirror (画文帯神獣鏡), were found in disturbed soil within the chamber. === Mound Structure === The mound, initially covered in bamboo, had been heavily modified by later activities. Excavations revealed that the mound, originally believed to be keyhole-shaped, is scallop-shaped. It consists of three tiers and measures: * Total length: 51 meters * Diameter of the rear section: 38.4 meters * Length of the frontal section: 12.5 meters * Width of the frontal section: Approximately 31 meters * Height of the rear section: Over 6.5 meters The mound was covered with fukiishi (葺石), sourced locally within 500 meters. The tiers were encircled with cylindrical haniwa, along with jar- and lid-shaped haniwa. Seven lid-shaped haniwa were uncovered across three excavations. === Construction Period === Based on these findings, archaeologist {{ill|Hiroshi Tsude|ja|都出比呂志}} estimates the mound's construction to around the late 4th century to early 5th century. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Toriimae Kofun (part of the Otokuni Kofun Group) * Designated on March 1, 2016, along with other mounds in the Otokuni Kofun Group, including {{ill|Tenno no Mori Kofun|ja|天皇の杜古墳}}, {{ill|Egayama Kofun|ja|恵解山古墳}}, and {{ill|Terado Otsuka Kofun|ja|寺戸大塚古墳}}.2016 Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31 == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|last=Tsude|first=Hiroshi|authorlink=Hiroshi Tsude|title=Toriimae Kofun - Comprehensive Report|publisher=Osaka University Archaeological Research Laboratory|year=1990|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=Ōyamazaki Town Board of Education|title=Historic Site Otokuni Kofun Group Toriimae Kofun|url=http://www.town.oyamazaki.kyoto.jp/material/files/group/51/ri-fu.pdf|publisher=Ōyamazaki Town|year=2019|ref=harv}} == External Links == * [http://www.isekiwalker.com/iseki/108736/ Toriimae Kofun] - Iseki Walker β [[Category:Otokuni Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Ōyamazaki Town History]] [[Category:Scallop-shaped Kofun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Kyoto]] [[Nagahouji-Nambahara Kofun]] |{{ill|Torii-mae Kofun|ja|鳥居前古墳}} ... 8 KB (1,031 words) - 20:59, 15 January[[Category:original pages]][[Category:most recent 100 pages]][[Category:kofun stubs]] 2025 Scallop-shaped Kofun * {{ill|Torii Mae Kofun|ja|鳥居前古墳}}

revision.text:
{{nihongo|'''Torii-mae Kofun'''|[[:ja:鳥居前古墳|鳥居前古墳]]}} is a scallop-shaped burial mound (帆立貝形古墳) located in {{ill|Ōyamazaki Town|ja|大山崎町}}, Otokuni District, Kyoto Prefecture. It is designated as a National Historic Site as part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Group|ja|乙訓古墳群}}. The mound was discovered in a 1967 survey and has since undergone three archaeological investigations by Kyoto Prefecture and Osaka University. == Overview == Toriimae Kofun is situated on the northern slopes of Mount Tennozan, known for the famous Battle of Yamazaki, at an elevation of 97 meters. The burial mound was identified during a distribution survey conducted by the Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education in 1967. Excavations of the rear circular section and part of the mound were conducted in 1969. Initially thought to be a keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳), subsequent investigations revised the designation to a scallop-shaped mound. === Burial Facility and Grave Goods === The rear circular section contains a single vertical stone chamber (竪穴式石室), oriented diagonally to the main axis of the mound. Measurements are as follows: * Burial chamber: 9.0 × 4.4 meters * Stone chamber: Length 6.5 meters, width 1.2 meters (west end), 0.77 meters (east end) The chamber's upper stones and ceiling stones were already missing. A clay bed within the chamber likely housed a split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin (割竹形木棺) of 5.3 meters in length. The coffin bed contained: * 2 magatama * 23 cylindrical beads (管玉) * 8 iron swords * 21 daggers * 9 short swords * 2 comma-shaped copper objects (巴形銅器) Additional grave goods, including swords, arrows, armor fragments, and fragments of a deity-beast mirror (画文帯神獣鏡), were found in disturbed soil within the chamber. === Mound Structure === The mound, initially covered in bamboo, had been heavily modified by later activities. Excavations revealed that the mound, originally believed to be keyhole-shaped, is scallop-shaped. It consists of three tiers and measures: * Total length: 51 meters * Diameter of the rear section: 38.4 meters * Length of the frontal section: 12.5 meters * Width of the frontal section: Approximately 31 meters * Height of the rear section: Over 6.5 meters The mound was covered with fukiishi (葺石), sourced locally within 500 meters. The tiers were encircled with cylindrical haniwa, along with jar- and lid-shaped haniwa. Seven lid-shaped haniwa were uncovered across three excavations. === Construction Period === Based on these findings, archaeologist {{ill|Hiroshi Tsude|ja|都出比呂志}} estimates the mound's construction to around the late 4th century to early 5th century. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Toriimae Kofun (part of the Otokuni Kofun Group) * Designated on March 1, 2016, along with other mounds in the Otokuni Kofun Group, including {{ill|Tenno no Mori Kofun|ja|天皇の杜古墳}}, {{ill|Egayama Kofun|ja|恵解山古墳}}, and {{ill|Terado Otsuka Kofun|ja|寺戸大塚古墳}}.2016 Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31 == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|last=Tsude|first=Hiroshi|authorlink=Hiroshi Tsude|title=Toriimae Kofun - Comprehensive Report|publisher=Osaka University Archaeological Research Laboratory|year=1990|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=Ōyamazaki Town Board of Education|title=Historic Site Otokuni Kofun Group Toriimae Kofun|url=http://www.town.oyamazaki.kyoto.jp/material/files/group/51/ri-fu.pdf|publisher=Ōyamazaki Town|year=2019|ref=harv}} == External Links == * [http://www.isekiwalker.com/iseki/108736/ Toriimae Kofun] - Iseki Walker β [[Category:Otokuni Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Ōyamazaki Town History]] [[Category:Scallop-shaped Kofun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Kyoto]] [[Nagahouji-Nambahara Kofun]] |{{ill|Torii-mae Kofun|ja|鳥居前古墳}} ... 8 KB (1,031 words) - 20:59, 15 January[[Category:original pages]][[Category:most recent 100 pages]][[Category:kofun stubs]] 2025 Scallop-shaped Kofun * {{ill|Torii Mae Kofun|ja|鳥居前古墳}}

Seven lid-shaped haniwa were uncovered across three excavations. === Construction Period === Based on these findings, archaeologist {{ill|Hiroshi Tsude|ja|都出比呂志}} estimates the mound's construction to around the late 4th century to early 5th century. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Toriimae Kofun (part of the Otokuni Kofun Group) * Designated on March 1, 2016, along with other mounds in the Otokuni Kofun Group, including {{ill|Tenno no Mori Kofun|ja|天皇の杜古墳}}, {{ill|Egayama Kofun|ja|恵解山古墳}}, and {{ill|Terado Otsuka Kofun|ja|寺戸大塚古墳}}.2016 Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31 == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|last=Tsude|first=Hiroshi|authorlink=Hiroshi Tsude|title=Toriimae Kofun - Comprehensive Report|publisher=Osaka University Archaeological Research Laboratory|year=1990|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=Ōyamazaki Town Board of Education|title=Historic Site Otokuni Kofun Group Toriimae Kofun|url=http://www.town.oyamazaki.kyoto.jp/material/files/group/51/ri-fu.pdf|publisher=Ōyamazaki Town|year=2019|ref=harv}} == External Links == * [http://www.isekiwalker.com/iseki/108736/ Toriimae Kofun] - Iseki Walker β [[Category:Otokuni Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Ōyamazaki Town History]] [[Category:Scallop-shaped Kofun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Kyoto]] [[Nagahouji-Nambahara Kofun]] |{{ill|Torii-mae Kofun|ja|鳥居前古墳}} ... 8 KB (1,031 words) - 20:59, 15 January[[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] 2025 Scallop-shaped Kofun * {{ill|Torii Mae Kofun|ja|鳥居前古墳}}

revision.text:
{{nihongo|'''Torii-mae Kofun'''|[[:ja:鳥居前古墳|鳥居前古墳]]}} is a scallop-shaped burial mound (帆立貝形古墳) located in {{ill|Ōyamazaki Town|ja|大山崎町}}, Otokuni District, Kyoto Prefecture. It is designated as a National Historic Site as part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Group|ja|乙訓古墳群}}. The mound was discovered in a 1967 survey and has since undergone three archaeological investigations by Kyoto Prefecture and Osaka University. == Overview == Toriimae Kofun is situated on the northern slopes of Mount Tennozan, known for the famous Battle of Yamazaki, at an elevation of 97 meters. The burial mound was identified during a distribution survey conducted by the Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education in 1967. Excavations of the rear circular section and part of the mound were conducted in 1969. Initially thought to be a keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳), subsequent investigations revised the designation to a scallop-shaped mound. === Burial Facility and Grave Goods === The rear circular section contains a single vertical stone chamber (竪穴式石室), oriented diagonally to the main axis of the mound. Measurements are as follows: * Burial chamber: 9.0 × 4.4 meters * Stone chamber: Length 6.5 meters, width 1.2 meters (west end), 0.77 meters (east end) The chamber's upper stones and ceiling stones were already missing. A clay bed within the chamber likely housed a split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin (割竹形木棺) of 5.3 meters in length. The coffin bed contained: * 2 magatama * 23 cylindrical beads (管玉) * 8 iron swords * 21 daggers * 9 short swords * 2 comma-shaped copper objects (巴形銅器) Additional grave goods, including swords, arrows, armor fragments, and fragments of a deity-beast mirror (画文帯神獣鏡), were found in disturbed soil within the chamber. === Mound Structure === The mound, initially covered in bamboo, had been heavily modified by later activities. Excavations revealed that the mound, originally believed to be keyhole-shaped, is scallop-shaped. It consists of three tiers and measures: * Total length: 51 meters * Diameter of the rear section: 38.4 meters * Length of the frontal section: 12.5 meters * Width of the frontal section: Approximately 31 meters * Height of the rear section: Over 6.5 meters The mound was covered with fukiishi (葺石), sourced locally within 500 meters. The tiers were encircled with cylindrical haniwa, along with jar- and lid-shaped haniwa. Seven lid-shaped haniwa were uncovered across three excavations. === Construction Period === Based on these findings, archaeologist {{ill|Hiroshi Tsude|ja|都出比呂志}} estimates the mound's construction to around the late 4th century to early 5th century. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Toriimae Kofun (part of the Otokuni Kofun Group) * Designated on March 1, 2016, along with other mounds in the Otokuni Kofun Group, including {{ill|Tenno no Mori Kofun|ja|天皇の杜古墳}}, {{ill|Egayama Kofun|ja|恵解山古墳}}, and {{ill|Terado Otsuka Kofun|ja|寺戸大塚古墳}}.2016 Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31 == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|last=Tsude|first=Hiroshi|authorlink=Hiroshi Tsude|title=Toriimae Kofun - Comprehensive Report|publisher=Osaka University Archaeological Research Laboratory|year=1990|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=Ōyamazaki Town Board of Education|title=Historic Site Otokuni Kofun Group Toriimae Kofun|url=http://www.town.oyamazaki.kyoto.jp/material/files/group/51/ri-fu.pdf|publisher=Ōyamazaki Town|year=2019|ref=harv}} == External Links == * [http://www.isekiwalker.com/iseki/108736/ Toriimae Kofun] - Iseki Walker β [[Category:Otokuni Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Ōyamazaki Town History]] [[Category:Scallop-shaped Kofun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Kyoto]] [[Nagahouji-Nambahara Kofun]] |{{ill|Torii-mae Kofun|ja|鳥居前古墳}} ... 8 KB (1,031 words) - 20:59, 15 January[[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] 2025 Scallop-shaped Kofun * {{ill|Torii Mae Kofun|ja|鳥居前古墳}}

Seven lid-shaped haniwa were uncovered across three excavations. === Construction Period === Based on these findings, archaeologist {{ill|Hiroshi Tsude|ja|都出比呂志}} estimates the mound's construction to around the late 4th century to early 5th century. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Toriimae Kofun (part of the Otokuni Kofun Group) * Designated on March 1, 2016, along with other mounds in the Otokuni Kofun Group, including {{ill|Tenno no Mori Kofun|ja|天皇の杜古墳}}, {{ill|Egayama Kofun|ja|恵解山古墳}}, and {{ill|Terado Otsuka Kofun|ja|寺戸大塚古墳}}.2016 Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31 == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|last=Tsude|first=Hiroshi|authorlink=Hiroshi Tsude|title=Toriimae Kofun - Comprehensive Report|publisher=Osaka University Archaeological Research Laboratory|year=1990|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=Ōyamazaki Town Board of Education|title=Historic Site Otokuni Kofun Group Toriimae Kofun|url=http://www.town.oyamazaki.kyoto.jp/material/files/group/51/ri-fu.pdf|publisher=Ōyamazaki Town|year=2019|ref=harv}} == External Links == * [http://www.isekiwalker.com/iseki/108736/ Toriimae Kofun] - Iseki Walker β [[Category:Otokuni Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Ōyamazaki Town History]] [[Category:Scallop-shaped Kofun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Kyoto]] [[Nagahouji-Nambahara Kofun]] |{{ill|Torii-mae Kofun|ja|鳥居前古墳}} ... 8 KB (1,031 words) - 20:59, 15 January[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] 2025 Scallop-shaped Kofun * {{ill|Torii Mae Kofun|ja|鳥居前古墳}}

{{nihongo|'''Torii-mae Kofun'''|[[:ja:鳥居前古墳|鳥居前古墳]]]] is a scallop-shaped burial mound (帆立貝形古墳) located in {{ill|Ōyamazaki Town|en]][[ja:大山崎町]], Otokuni District, Kyoto Prefecture. It is designated as a National Historic Site as part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Group|en]][[ja:乙訓古墳群]]. The mound was discovered in a 1967 survey and has since undergone three archaeological investigations by Kyoto Prefecture and Osaka University. == Overview == Toriimae Kofun is situated on the northern slopes of Mount Tennozan, known for the famous Battle of Yamazaki, at an elevation of 97 meters. The burial mound was identified during a distribution survey conducted by the Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education in 1967. Excavations of the rear circular section and part of the mound were conducted in 1969. Initially thought to be a keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳), subsequent investigations revised the designation to a scallop-shaped mound. === Burial Facility and Grave Goods === The rear circular section contains a single vertical stone chamber (竪穴式石室), oriented diagonally to the main axis of the mound. Measurements are as follows: * Burial chamber: 9.0 × 4.4 meters * Stone chamber: Length 6.5 meters, width 1.2 meters (west end), 0.77 meters (east end) The chamber's upper stones and ceiling stones were already missing. A clay bed within the chamber likely housed a split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin (割竹形木棺) of 5.3 meters in length. The coffin bed contained: * 2 magatama * 23 cylindrical beads (管玉) * 8 iron swords * 21 daggers * 9 short swords * 2 comma-shaped copper objects (巴形銅器) Additional grave goods, including swords, arrows, armor fragments, and fragments of a deity-beast mirror (画文帯神獣鏡), were found in disturbed soil within the chamber. === Mound Structure === The mound, initially covered in bamboo, had been heavily modified by later activities. Excavations revealed that the mound, originally believed to be keyhole-shaped, is scallop-shaped. It consists of three tiers and measures: * Total length: 51 meters * Diameter of the rear section: 38.4 meters * Length of the frontal section: 12.5 meters * Width of the frontal section: Approximately 31 meters * Height of the rear section: Over 6.5 meters The mound was covered with fukiishi (葺石), sourced locally within 500 meters. The tiers were encircled with cylindrical haniwa, along with jar- and lid-shaped haniwa. Seven lid-shaped haniwa were uncovered across three excavations. === Construction Period === Based on these findings, archaeologist {{ill|Hiroshi Tsude|en]][[ja:都出比呂志]] estimates the mound's construction to around the late 4th century to early 5th century. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Toriimae Kofun (part of the Otokuni Kofun Group) * Designated on March 1, 2016, along with other mounds in the Otokuni Kofun Group, including {{ill|Tenno no Mori Kofun|en]][[ja:天皇の杜古墳]], {{ill|Egayama Kofun|en]][[ja:恵解山古墳]], and {{ill|Terado Otsuka Kofun|en]][[ja:寺戸大塚古墳]].2016 Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31 == References == {{脚注ヘルプ]] {{Reflist]] == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|last=Tsude|first=Hiroshi|authorlink=Hiroshi Tsude|title=Toriimae Kofun - Comprehensive Report|publisher=Osaka University Archaeological Research Laboratory|year=1990|ref=harv]] * {{Cite book|和書|author=Ōyamazaki Town Board of Education|title=Historic Site Otokuni Kofun Group Toriimae Kofun|url=http://www.town.oyamazaki.kyoto.jp/material/files/group/51/ri-fu.pdf|publisher=Ōyamazaki Town|year=2019|ref=harv]] == External Links == * [http://www.isekiwalker.com/iseki/108736/ Toriimae Kofun] - Iseki Walker β [[Category:Otokuni Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Ōyamazaki Town History]] [[Category:Scallop-shaped Kofun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Kyoto]] [[Nagahouji-Nambahara Kofun]] |{{ill|Torii-mae Kofun|en]][[ja:鳥居前古墳]] ... 8 KB (1,031 words) - 20:59, 15 January[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] 2025 Scallop-shaped Kofun * {{ill|Torii Mae Kofun|en]][[ja:鳥居前古墳]]

Seven lid-shaped haniwa were uncovered across three excavations. === Construction Period === Based on these findings, archaeologist {{ill|Hiroshi Tsude|en}}[[ja:都出比呂志]] estimates the mound's construction to around the late 4th century to early 5th century. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Toriimae Kofun (part of the Otokuni Kofun Group) * Designated on March 1, 2016, along with other mounds in the Otokuni Kofun Group, including {{ill|Tenno no Mori Kofun|en}}[[ja:天皇の杜古墳]], {{ill|Egayama Kofun|en}}[[ja:恵解山古墳]], and {{ill|Terado Otsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:寺戸大塚古墳]].2016 Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31 == References == {{脚注ヘルプ]] {{Reflist]] == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|last=Tsude|first=Hiroshi|authorlink=Hiroshi Tsude|title=Toriimae Kofun - Comprehensive Report|publisher=Osaka University Archaeological Research Laboratory|year=1990|ref=harv]] * {{Cite book|和書|author=Ōyamazaki Town Board of Education|title=Historic Site Otokuni Kofun Group Toriimae Kofun|url=http://www.town.oyamazaki.kyoto.jp/material/files/group/51/ri-fu.pdf|publisher=Ōyamazaki Town|year=2019|ref=harv]] == External Links == * [http://www.isekiwalker.com/iseki/108736/ Toriimae Kofun] - Iseki Walker β [[Category:Otokuni Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Ōyamazaki Town History]] [[Category:Scallop-shaped Kofun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Kyoto]] [[Nagahouji-Nambahara Kofun]] |{{ill|Torii-mae Kofun|en}}[[ja:鳥居前古墳]] ... 8 KB (1,031 words) - 20:59, 15 January[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] 2025 Scallop-shaped Kofun * {{ill|Torii Mae Kofun|en}}[[ja:鳥居前古墳]]

revision.text:
{{nihongo|'''Torii-mae Kofun'''|[[:ja:鳥居前古墳|鳥居前古墳]]]] is a scallop-shaped burial mound (帆立貝形古墳) located in {{ill|Ōyamazaki Town|en}}[[ja:大山崎町]], Otokuni District, Kyoto Prefecture. It is designated as a National Historic Site as part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Group|en}}[[ja:乙訓古墳群]]. The mound was discovered in a 1967 survey and has since undergone three archaeological investigations by Kyoto Prefecture and Osaka University. == Overview == Toriimae Kofun is situated on the northern slopes of Mount Tennozan, known for the famous Battle of Yamazaki, at an elevation of 97 meters. The burial mound was identified during a distribution survey conducted by the Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education in 1967. Excavations of the rear circular section and part of the mound were conducted in 1969. Initially thought to be a keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳), subsequent investigations revised the designation to a scallop-shaped mound. === Burial Facility and Grave Goods === The rear circular section contains a single vertical stone chamber (竪穴式石室), oriented diagonally to the main axis of the mound. Measurements are as follows: * Burial chamber: 9.0 × 4.4 meters * Stone chamber: Length 6.5 meters, width 1.2 meters (west end), 0.77 meters (east end) The chamber's upper stones and ceiling stones were already missing. A clay bed within the chamber likely housed a split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin (割竹形木棺) of 5.3 meters in length. The coffin bed contained: * 2 magatama * 23 cylindrical beads (管玉) * 8 iron swords * 21 daggers * 9 short swords * 2 comma-shaped copper objects (巴形銅器) Additional grave goods, including swords, arrows, armor fragments, and fragments of a deity-beast mirror (画文帯神獣鏡), were found in disturbed soil within the chamber. === Mound Structure === The mound, initially covered in bamboo, had been heavily modified by later activities. Excavations revealed that the mound, originally believed to be keyhole-shaped, is scallop-shaped. It consists of three tiers and measures: * Total length: 51 meters * Diameter of the rear section: 38.4 meters * Length of the frontal section: 12.5 meters * Width of the frontal section: Approximately 31 meters * Height of the rear section: Over 6.5 meters The mound was covered with fukiishi (葺石), sourced locally within 500 meters. The tiers were encircled with cylindrical haniwa, along with jar- and lid-shaped haniwa. Seven lid-shaped haniwa were uncovered across three excavations. === Construction Period === Based on these findings, archaeologist {{ill|Hiroshi Tsude|en}}[[ja:都出比呂志]] estimates the mound's construction to around the late 4th century to early 5th century. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Toriimae Kofun (part of the Otokuni Kofun Group) * Designated on March 1, 2016, along with other mounds in the Otokuni Kofun Group, including {{ill|Tenno no Mori Kofun|en}}[[ja:天皇の杜古墳]], {{ill|Egayama Kofun|en}}[[ja:恵解山古墳]], and {{ill|Terado Otsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:寺戸大塚古墳]].2016 Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31 == References == {{脚注ヘルプ]] {{Reflist]] == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|last=Tsude|first=Hiroshi|authorlink=Hiroshi Tsude|title=Toriimae Kofun - Comprehensive Report|publisher=Osaka University Archaeological Research Laboratory|year=1990|ref=harv]] * {{Cite book|和書|author=Ōyamazaki Town Board of Education|title=Historic Site Otokuni Kofun Group Toriimae Kofun|url=http://www.town.oyamazaki.kyoto.jp/material/files/group/51/ri-fu.pdf|publisher=Ōyamazaki Town|year=2019|ref=harv]] == External Links == * [http://www.isekiwalker.com/iseki/108736/ Toriimae Kofun] - Iseki Walker β [[Category:Otokuni Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Ōyamazaki Town History]] [[Category:Scallop-shaped Kofun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Kyoto]] [[Nagahouji-Nambahara Kofun]] |{{ill|Torii-mae Kofun|en}}[[ja:鳥居前古墳]] ... 8 KB (1,031 words) - 20:59, 15 January[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] 2025 Scallop-shaped Kofun * {{ill|Torii Mae Kofun|en}}[[ja:鳥居前古墳]]

Seven lid-shaped haniwa were uncovered across three excavations. === Construction Period === Based on these findings, archaeologist {{ill|Hiroshi Tsude|en}}[[ja:都出比呂志]] estimates the mound's construction to around the late 4th century to early 5th century. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Toriimae Kofun (part of the Otokuni Kofun Group) * Designated on March 1, 2016, along with other mounds in the Otokuni Kofun Group, including {{ill|Tenno-no-Mori Kofun|en}}[[ja:天皇の杜古墳]], {{ill|Egayama Kofun|en}}[[ja:恵解山古墳]], and {{ill|Terado Otsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:寺戸大塚古墳]].2016 Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31 == References == {{脚注ヘルプ]] {{Reflist]] == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|last=Tsude|first=Hiroshi|authorlink=Hiroshi Tsude|title=Toriimae Kofun - Comprehensive Report|publisher=Osaka University Archaeological Research Laboratory|year=1990|ref=harv]] * {{Cite book|和書|author=Ōyamazaki Town Board of Education|title=Historic Site Otokuni Kofun Group Toriimae Kofun|url=http://www.town.oyamazaki.kyoto.jp/material/files/group/51/ri-fu.pdf|publisher=Ōyamazaki Town|year=2019|ref=harv]] == External Links == * [http://www.isekiwalker.com/iseki/108736/ Toriimae Kofun] - Iseki Walker β [[Category:Otokuni Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Ōyamazaki Town History]] [[Category:Scallop-shaped Kofun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Kyoto]] [[Nagahouji-Nambahara Kofun]] |{{ill|Torii-mae Kofun|en}}[[ja:鳥居前古墳]] ... 8 KB (1,031 words) - 20:59, 15 January [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] 2025 Scallop-shaped Kofun * {{ill|Torii Mae Kofun|en}}[[ja:鳥居前古墳]]

revision.text:
{{nihongo|'''Torii-mae Kofun'''|[[:ja:鳥居前古墳|鳥居前古墳]]}} is a scallop-shaped burial mound (帆立貝形古墳) located in {{ill|Ōyamazaki Town|en}}[[ja:大山崎町]], Otokuni District, Kyoto Prefecture. It is designated as a National Historic Site as part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Group|en}}[[ja:乙訓古墳群]]. The mound was discovered in a 1967 survey and has since undergone three archaeological investigations by Kyoto Prefecture and Osaka University. == Overview == Toriimae Kofun is situated on the northern slopes of Mount Tennozan, known for the famous Battle of Yamazaki, at an elevation of 97 meters. The burial mound was identified during a distribution survey conducted by the Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education in 1967. Excavations of the rear circular section and part of the mound were conducted in 1969. Initially thought to be a keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳), subsequent investigations revised the designation to a scallop-shaped mound. === Burial Facility and Grave Goods === The rear circular section contains a single vertical stone chamber (竪穴式石室), oriented diagonally to the main axis of the mound. Measurements are as follows: * Burial chamber: 9.0 × 4.4 meters * Stone chamber: Length 6.5 meters, width 1.2 meters (west end), 0.77 meters (east end) The chamber's upper stones and ceiling stones were already missing. A clay bed within the chamber likely housed a split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin (割竹形木棺) of 5.3 meters in length. The coffin bed contained: * 2 magatama * 23 cylindrical beads (管玉) * 8 iron swords * 21 daggers * 9 short swords * 2 comma-shaped copper objects (巴形銅器) Additional grave goods, including swords, arrows, armor fragments, and fragments of a deity-beast mirror (画文帯神獣鏡), were found in disturbed soil within the chamber. === Mound Structure === The mound, initially covered in bamboo, had been heavily modified by later activities. Excavations revealed that the mound, originally believed to be keyhole-shaped, is scallop-shaped. It consists of three tiers and measures: * Total length: 51 meters * Diameter of the rear section: 38.4 meters * Length of the frontal section: 12.5 meters * Width of the frontal section: Approximately 31 meters * Height of the rear section: Over 6.5 meters The mound was covered with fukiishi (葺石), sourced locally within 500 meters. The tiers were encircled with cylindrical haniwa, along with jar- and lid-shaped haniwa. Seven lid-shaped haniwa were uncovered across three excavations. === Construction Period === Based on these findings, archaeologist {{ill|Hiroshi Tsude|en}}[[ja:都出比呂志]] estimates the mound's construction to around the late 4th century to early 5th century. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Toriimae Kofun (part of the Otokuni Kofun Group) * Designated on March 1, 2016, along with other mounds in the Otokuni Kofun Group, including {{ill|Tenno-no-Mori Kofun|en}}[[ja:天皇の杜古墳]], {{ill|Egayama Kofun|en}}[[ja:恵解山古墳]], and {{ill|Terado Otsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:寺戸大塚古墳]].2016 Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31 == References == {{脚注ヘルプ]] {{Reflist]] == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|last=Tsude|first=Hiroshi|authorlink=Hiroshi Tsude|title=Toriimae Kofun - Comprehensive Report|publisher=Osaka University Archaeological Research Laboratory|year=1990|ref=harv]] * {{Cite book|和書|author=Ōyamazaki Town Board of Education|title=Historic Site Otokuni Kofun Group Toriimae Kofun|url=http://www.town.oyamazaki.kyoto.jp/material/files/group/51/ri-fu.pdf|publisher=Ōyamazaki Town|year=2019|ref=harv]] == External Links == * [http://www.isekiwalker.com/iseki/108736/ Toriimae Kofun] - Iseki Walker β [[Category:Otokuni Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Ōyamazaki Town History]] [[Category:Scallop-shaped Kofun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Kyoto]] [[Nagahouji-Nambahara Kofun]] |{{ill|Torii-mae Kofun|en}}[[ja:鳥居前古墳]] ... 8 KB (1,031 words) - 20:59, 15 January [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] 2025 Scallop-shaped Kofun * {{ill|Torii Mae Kofun|en}}[[ja:鳥居前古墳]]

Seven lid-shaped haniwa were uncovered across three excavations. === Construction Period === Based on these findings, archaeologist {{ill|Hiroshi Tsude|ja|都出比呂志}}[[ja:都出比呂志]] estimates the mound's construction to around the late 4th century to early 5th century. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Toriimae Kofun (part of the Otokuni Kofun Group) * Designated on March 1, 2016, along with other mounds in the Otokuni Kofun Group, including {{ill|Tenno-no-Mori Kofun|ja|天皇の杜古墳}}[[ja:天皇の杜古墳]], {{ill|Egayama Kofun|ja|恵解山古墳}}[[ja:恵解山古墳]], and {{ill|Terado Otsuka Kofun|ja|寺戸大塚古墳}}[[ja:寺戸大塚古墳]].2016 Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31 == References == {{脚注ヘルプ]] {{Reflist]] == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|last=Tsude|first=Hiroshi|authorlink=Hiroshi Tsude|title=Toriimae Kofun - Comprehensive Report|publisher=Osaka University Archaeological Research Laboratory|year=1990|ref=harv]] * {{Cite book|和書|author=Ōyamazaki Town Board of Education|title=Historic Site Otokuni Kofun Group Toriimae Kofun|url=http://www.town.oyamazaki.kyoto.jp/material/files/group/51/ri-fu.pdf|publisher=Ōyamazaki Town|year=2019|ref=harv]] == External Links == * [http://www.isekiwalker.com/iseki/108736/ Toriimae Kofun] - Iseki Walker β [[Category:Otokuni Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Ōyamazaki Town History]] [[Category:Scallop-shaped Kofun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Kyoto]] [[Nagahouji-Nambahara Kofun]] |{{ill|Torii-mae Kofun|en}}[[ja:鳥居前古墳]] ... 8 KB (1,031 words) - 20:59, 15 January [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Toriimae Kofun]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] 2025 Scallop-shaped Kofun * {{ill|Torii Mae Kofun|en}}[[ja:鳥居前古墳]]

revision.text:
{{nihongo|'''Torii-mae Kofun'''|[[:ja:鳥居前古墳|鳥居前古墳]][[ja:鳥居前古墳]]}} is a scallop-shaped burial mound (帆立貝形古墳) located in {{ill|Ōyamazaki Town|ja|大山崎町}}[[ja:大山崎町]], Otokuni District, Kyoto Prefecture. It is designated as a National Historic Site as part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Group|ja|乙訓古墳群}}[[ja:乙訓古墳群]]. The mound was discovered in a 1967 survey and has since undergone three archaeological investigations by Kyoto Prefecture and Osaka University. == Overview == Toriimae Kofun is situated on the northern slopes of Mount Tennozan, known for the famous Battle of Yamazaki, at an elevation of 97 meters. The burial mound was identified during a distribution survey conducted by the Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education in 1967. Excavations of the rear circular section and part of the mound were conducted in 1969. Initially thought to be a keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳), subsequent investigations revised the designation to a scallop-shaped mound. === Burial Facility and Grave Goods === The rear circular section contains a single vertical stone chamber (竪穴式石室), oriented diagonally to the main axis of the mound. Measurements are as follows: * Burial chamber: 9.0 × 4.4 meters * Stone chamber: Length 6.5 meters, width 1.2 meters (west end), 0.77 meters (east end) The chamber's upper stones and ceiling stones were already missing. A clay bed within the chamber likely housed a split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin (割竹形木棺) of 5.3 meters in length. The coffin bed contained: * 2 magatama * 23 cylindrical beads (管玉) * 8 iron swords * 21 daggers * 9 short swords * 2 comma-shaped copper objects (巴形銅器) Additional grave goods, including swords, arrows, armor fragments, and fragments of a deity-beast mirror (画文帯神獣鏡), were found in disturbed soil within the chamber. === Mound Structure === The mound, initially covered in bamboo, had been heavily modified by later activities. Excavations revealed that the mound, originally believed to be keyhole-shaped, is scallop-shaped. It consists of three tiers and measures: * Total length: 51 meters * Diameter of the rear section: 38.4 meters * Length of the frontal section: 12.5 meters * Width of the frontal section: Approximately 31 meters * Height of the rear section: Over 6.5 meters The mound was covered with fukiishi (葺石), sourced locally within 500 meters. The tiers were encircled with cylindrical haniwa, along with jar- and lid-shaped haniwa. Seven lid-shaped haniwa were uncovered across three excavations. === Construction Period === Based on these findings, archaeologist {{ill|Hiroshi Tsude|ja|都出比呂志}}[[ja:都出比呂志]] estimates the mound's construction to around the late 4th century to early 5th century. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Toriimae Kofun (part of the Otokuni Kofun Group) * Designated on March 1, 2016, along with other mounds in the Otokuni Kofun Group, including {{ill|Tenno-no-Mori Kofun|ja|天皇の杜古墳}}[[ja:天皇の杜古墳]], {{ill|Egayama Kofun|ja|恵解山古墳}}[[ja:恵解山古墳]], and {{ill|Terado Otsuka Kofun|ja|寺戸大塚古墳}}[[ja:寺戸大塚古墳]].2016 Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31 == References == {{脚注ヘルプ]] {{Reflist]] == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|last=Tsude|first=Hiroshi|authorlink=Hiroshi Tsude|title=Toriimae Kofun - Comprehensive Report|publisher=Osaka University Archaeological Research Laboratory|year=1990|ref=harv]] * {{Cite book|和書|author=Ōyamazaki Town Board of Education|title=Historic Site Otokuni Kofun Group Toriimae Kofun|url=http://www.town.oyamazaki.kyoto.jp/material/files/group/51/ri-fu.pdf|publisher=Ōyamazaki Town|year=2019|ref=harv]] == External Links == * [http://www.isekiwalker.com/iseki/108736/ Toriimae Kofun] - Iseki Walker β [[Category:Otokuni Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Ōyamazaki Town History]] [[Category:Scallop-shaped Kofun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Kyoto]] [[Nagahouji-Nambahara Kofun]] |{{ill|Torii-mae Kofun|en}}[[ja:鳥居前古墳]] ... 8 KB (1,031 words) - 20:59, 15 January [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Toriimae Kofun]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] 2025 Scallop-shaped Kofun * {{ill|Torii Mae Kofun|en}}[[ja:鳥居前古墳]]

{{nihongo|'''Torii-mae Kofun'''|[[:ja:鳥居前古墳|鳥居前古墳]][[ja:鳥居前古墳]]}} is a scallop-shaped burial mound (帆立貝形古墳) located in {{ill|Ōyamazaki Town|ja|大山崎町}}[[ja:大山崎町]], Otokuni District, Kyoto Prefecture. It is designated as a National Historic Site as part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Group|ja|乙訓古墳群}}[[ja:乙訓古墳群]]. The mound was discovered in a 1967 survey and has since undergone three archaeological investigations by Kyoto Prefecture and Osaka University. == Overview == Toriimae Kofun is situated on the northern slopes of Mount Tennozan, known for the famous Battle of Yamazaki, at an elevation of 97 meters. The burial mound was identified during a distribution survey conducted by the Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education in 1967. Excavations of the rear circular section and part of the mound were conducted in 1969. Initially thought to be a keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳), subsequent investigations revised the designation to a scallop-shaped mound. === Burial Facility and Grave Goods === The rear circular section contains a single vertical stone chamber (竪穴式石室), oriented diagonally to the main axis of the mound. Measurements are as follows: * Burial chamber: 9.0 × 4.4 meters * Stone chamber: Length 6.5 meters, width 1.2 meters (west end), 0.77 meters (east end) The chamber's upper stones and ceiling stones were already missing. A clay bed within the chamber likely housed a split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin (割竹形木棺) of 5.3 meters in length. The coffin bed contained: * 2 magatama * 23 cylindrical beads (管玉) * 8 iron swords * 21 daggers * 9 short swords * 2 comma-shaped copper objects (巴形銅器) Additional grave goods, including swords, arrows, armor fragments, and fragments of a deity-beast mirror (画文帯神獣鏡), were found in disturbed soil within the chamber. === Mound Structure === The mound, initially covered in bamboo, had been heavily modified by later activities. Excavations revealed that the mound, originally believed to be keyhole-shaped, is scallop-shaped. It consists of three tiers and measures: * Total length: 51 meters * Diameter of the rear section: 38.4 meters * Length of the frontal section: 12.5 meters * Width of the frontal section: Approximately 31 meters * Height of the rear section: Over 6.5 meters The mound was covered with fukiishi (葺石), sourced locally within 500 meters. The tiers were encircled with cylindrical haniwa, along with jar- and lid-shaped haniwa. Seven lid-shaped haniwa were uncovered across three excavations. === Construction Period === Based on these findings, archaeologist {{ill|Hiroshi Tsude|ja|都出比呂志}}[[ja:都出比呂志]] estimates the mound's construction to around the late 4th century to early 5th century. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Toriimae Kofun (part of the Otokuni Kofun Group) * Designated on March 1, 2016, along with other mounds in the Otokuni Kofun Group, including {{ill|Tenno-no-Mori Kofun|ja|天皇の杜古墳}}[[ja:天皇の杜古墳]], {{ill|Egayama Kofun|ja|恵解山古墳}}[[ja:恵解山古墳]], and {{ill|Terado Otsuka Kofun|ja|寺戸大塚古墳}}[[ja:寺戸大塚古墳]].2016 Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31 == References == {{脚注ヘルプ]] {{Reflist]] == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|last=Tsude|first=Hiroshi|authorlink=Hiroshi Tsude|title=Toriimae Kofun - Comprehensive Report|publisher=Osaka University Archaeological Research Laboratory|year=1990|ref=harv]] * {{Cite book|和書|author=Ōyamazaki Town Board of Education|title=Historic Site Otokuni Kofun Group Toriimae Kofun|url=http://www.town.oyamazaki.kyoto.jp/material/files/group/51/ri-fu.pdf|publisher=Ōyamazaki Town|year=2019|ref=harv]] == External Links == * [http://www.isekiwalker.com/iseki/108736/ Toriimae Kofun] - Iseki Walker β [[Category:Otokuni Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Ōyamazaki Town History]] [[Category:Scallop-shaped Kofun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Kyoto]] [[Nagahouji-Nambahara Kofun]] |{{ill|Torii-mae Kofun|en}}[[ja:鳥居前古墳]] ... 8 KB (1,031 words) - 20:59, [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Toriimae Kofun-qq]] [[Category:Toriimae Kofun]][[Category:kofun stubs]] Scallop-shaped Kofun * {{ill|Torii Mae Kofun|en}}[[ja:鳥居前古墳]]

Seven lid-shaped haniwa were uncovered across three excavations. === Construction Period === Based on these findings, archaeologist {{ill|Hiroshi Tsude|ja|都出比呂志}}[[ja:都出比呂志]] estimates the mound's construction to around the late 4th century to early 5th century. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Toriimae Kofun (part of the Otokuni Kofun Group) * Designated on March 1, 2016, along with other mounds in the Otokuni Kofun Group, including {{ill|Tenno-no-Mori Kofun|ja|天皇の杜古墳}}[[ja:天皇の杜古墳]], {{ill|Egayama Kofun|ja|恵解山古墳}}[[ja:恵解山古墳]], and {{ill|Terado Otsuka Kofun|ja|寺戸大塚古墳}}[[ja:寺戸大塚古墳]].2016 Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31 == References == {{脚注ヘルプ]] {{Reflist]] == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|last=Tsude|first=Hiroshi|authorlink=Hiroshi Tsude|title=Toriimae Kofun - Comprehensive Report|publisher=Osaka University Archaeological Research Laboratory|year=1990|ref=harv]] * {{Cite book|和書|author=Ōyamazaki Town Board of Education|title=Historic Site Otokuni Kofun Group Toriimae Kofun|url=http://www.town.oyamazaki.kyoto.jp/material/files/group/51/ri-fu.pdf|publisher=Ōyamazaki Town|year=2019|ref=harv]] == External Links == * [http://www.isekiwalker.com/iseki/108736/ Toriimae Kofun] - Iseki Walker β [[Category:Otokuni Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Ōyamazaki Town History]] [[Category:Scallop-shaped Kofun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Kyoto]] [[Nagahouji-Nambahara Kofun]] |{{ill|Torii-mae Kofun|en}}[[ja:鳥居前古墳]] ... 8 KB (1,031 words) - 20:59, [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Toriimae Kofun-qq]] [[Category:Toriimae Kofun]][[Category:kofun stubs]] Scallop-shaped Kofun * {{ill|Torii Mae Kofun|en}}[[ja:鳥居前古墳]]

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{nihongo|'''Torii-mae Kofun'''|[[:ja:鳥居前古墳|鳥居前古墳]][[ja:鳥居前古墳]]}} is a scallop-shaped burial mound (帆立貝形古墳) located in {{ill|Ōyamazaki Town|ja|大山崎町}}[[ja:大山崎町]], Otokuni District, Kyoto Prefecture. It is designated as a National Historic Site as part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Group|ja|乙訓古墳群}}[[ja:乙訓古墳群]]. The mound was discovered in a 1967 survey and has since undergone three archaeological investigations by Kyoto Prefecture and Osaka University. == Overview == Toriimae Kofun is situated on the northern slopes of Mount Tennozan, known for the famous Battle of Yamazaki, at an elevation of 97 meters. The burial mound was identified during a distribution survey conducted by the Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education in 1967. Excavations of the rear circular section and part of the mound were conducted in 1969. Initially thought to be a keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳), subsequent investigations revised the designation to a scallop-shaped mound. === Burial Facility and Grave Goods === The rear circular section contains a single vertical stone chamber (竪穴式石室), oriented diagonally to the main axis of the mound. Measurements are as follows: * Burial chamber: 9.0 × 4.4 meters * Stone chamber: Length 6.5 meters, width 1.2 meters (west end), 0.77 meters (east end) The chamber's upper stones and ceiling stones were already missing. A clay bed within the chamber likely housed a split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin (割竹形木棺) of 5.3 meters in length. The coffin bed contained: * 2 magatama * 23 cylindrical beads (管玉) * 8 iron swords * 21 daggers * 9 short swords * 2 comma-shaped copper objects (巴形銅器) Additional grave goods, including swords, arrows, armor fragments, and fragments of a deity-beast mirror (画文帯神獣鏡), were found in disturbed soil within the chamber. === Mound Structure === The mound, initially covered in bamboo, had been heavily modified by later activities. Excavations revealed that the mound, originally believed to be keyhole-shaped, is scallop-shaped. It consists of three tiers and measures: * Total length: 51 meters * Diameter of the rear section: 38.4 meters * Length of the frontal section: 12.5 meters * Width of the frontal section: Approximately 31 meters * Height of the rear section: Over 6.5 meters The mound was covered with fukiishi (葺石), sourced locally within 500 meters. The tiers were encircled with cylindrical haniwa, along with jar- and lid-shaped haniwa. Seven lid-shaped haniwa were uncovered across three excavations. === Construction Period === Based on these findings, archaeologist {{ill|Hiroshi Tsude|ja|都出比呂志}}[[ja:都出比呂志]] estimates the mound's construction to around the late 4th century to early 5th century. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Toriimae Kofun (part of the Otokuni Kofun Group) * Designated on March 1, 2016, along with other mounds in the Otokuni Kofun Group, including {{ill|Tenno-no-Mori Kofun|ja|天皇の杜古墳}}[[ja:天皇の杜古墳]], {{ill|Egayama Kofun|ja|恵解山古墳}}[[ja:恵解山古墳]], and {{ill|Terado Otsuka Kofun|ja|寺戸大塚古墳}}[[ja:寺戸大塚古墳]].2016 Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31 == References == {{脚注ヘルプ]] {{Reflist]] == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|last=Tsude|first=Hiroshi|authorlink=Hiroshi Tsude|title=Toriimae Kofun - Comprehensive Report|publisher=Osaka University Archaeological Research Laboratory|year=1990|ref=harv]] * {{Cite book|和書|author=Ōyamazaki Town Board of Education|title=Historic Site Otokuni Kofun Group Toriimae Kofun|url=http://www.town.oyamazaki.kyoto.jp/material/files/group/51/ri-fu.pdf|publisher=Ōyamazaki Town|year=2019|ref=harv]] == External Links == * [http://www.isekiwalker.com/iseki/108736/ Toriimae Kofun] - Iseki Walker β [[Category:Otokuni Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Ōyamazaki Town History]] [[Category:Scallop-shaped Kofun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Kyoto]] [[Nagahouji-Nambahara Kofun]] |{{ill|Torii-mae Kofun|en}}[[ja:鳥居前古墳]] ... 8 KB (1,031 words) - 20:59, [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Toriimae Kofun-qq]] [[Category:Toriimae Kofun]][[Category:kofun stubs]] Scallop-shaped Kofun * {{ill|Torii Mae Kofun|en}}[[ja:鳥居前古墳]]

Seven lid-shaped haniwa were uncovered across three excavations. === Construction Period === Based on these findings, archaeologist {{ill|Hiroshi Tsude|ja|都出比呂志}}[[ja:都出比呂志]] estimates the mound's construction to around the late 4th century to early 5th century. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Toriimae Kofun (part of the Otokuni Kofun Group) * Designated on March 1, 2016, along with other mounds in the Otokuni Kofun Group, including {{ill|Tenno-no-Mori Kofun|ja|天皇の杜古墳}}[[ja:天皇の杜古墳]], {{ill|Egayama Kofun|ja|恵解山古墳}}[[ja:恵解山古墳]], and {{ill|Terado Otsuka Kofun|ja|寺戸大塚古墳}}[[ja:寺戸大塚古墳]].2016 Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31 == References == {{脚注ヘルプ]] {{Reflist]] == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|last=Tsude|first=Hiroshi|authorlink=Hiroshi Tsude|title=Toriimae Kofun - Comprehensive Report|publisher=Osaka University Archaeological Research Laboratory|year=1990|ref=harv]] * {{Cite book|和書|author=Ōyamazaki Town Board of Education|title=Historic Site Otokuni Kofun Group Toriimae Kofun|url=http://www.town.oyamazaki.kyoto.jp/material/files/group/51/ri-fu.pdf|publisher=Ōyamazaki Town|year=2019|ref=harv]] == External Links == * [http://www.isekiwalker.com/iseki/108736/ Toriimae Kofun] - Iseki Walker β [[Category:Otokuni Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Ōyamazaki Town History]] [[Category:Scallop-shaped Kofun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Kyoto]] [[Nagahouji-Nambahara Kofun]] |{{ill|Torii-mae Kofun|en}}[[ja:鳥居前古墳]] ... 8 KB (1,031 words) - 20:59, [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Toriimae Kofun-qq]] [[Category:Toriimae Kofun]][[Category:kofun stubs]] Scallop-shaped Kofun * {{ill|Torii Mae Kofun|en}}[[ja:鳥居前古墳]]

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:11]][[ja:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:11]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{nihongo|'''Torii-mae Kofun'''|[[:ja:鳥居前古墳|鳥居前古墳]][[ja:鳥居前古墳]]}} is a scallop-shaped burial mound (帆立貝形古墳) located in {{ill|Ōyamazaki Town|ja|大山崎町}}[[ja:大山崎町]], Otokuni District, Kyoto Prefecture. It is designated as a National Historic Site as part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Group|ja|乙訓古墳群}}[[ja:乙訓古墳群]]. The mound was discovered in a 1967 survey and has since undergone three archaeological investigations by Kyoto Prefecture and Osaka University. == Overview == Toriimae Kofun is situated on the northern slopes of Mount Tennozan, known for the famous Battle of Yamazaki, at an elevation of 97 meters. The burial mound was identified during a distribution survey conducted by the Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education in 1967. Excavations of the rear circular section and part of the mound were conducted in 1969. Initially thought to be a keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳), subsequent investigations revised the designation to a scallop-shaped mound. === Burial Facility and Grave Goods === The rear circular section contains a single vertical stone chamber (竪穴式石室), oriented diagonally to the main axis of the mound. Measurements are as follows: * Burial chamber: 9.0 × 4.4 meters * Stone chamber: Length 6.5 meters, width 1.2 meters (west end), 0.77 meters (east end) The chamber's upper stones and ceiling stones were already missing. A clay bed within the chamber likely housed a split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin (割竹形木棺) of 5.3 meters in length. The coffin bed contained: * 2 magatama * 23 cylindrical beads (管玉) * 8 iron swords * 21 daggers * 9 short swords * 2 comma-shaped copper objects (巴形銅器) Additional grave goods, including swords, arrows, armor fragments, and fragments of a deity-beast mirror (画文帯神獣鏡), were found in disturbed soil within the chamber. === Mound Structure === The mound, initially covered in bamboo, had been heavily modified by later activities. Excavations revealed that the mound, originally believed to be keyhole-shaped, is scallop-shaped. It consists of three tiers and measures: * Total length: 51 meters * Diameter of the rear section: 38.4 meters * Length of the frontal section: 12.5 meters * Width of the frontal section: Approximately 31 meters * Height of the rear section: Over 6.5 meters The mound was covered with fukiishi (葺石), sourced locally within 500 meters. The tiers were encircled with cylindrical haniwa, along with jar- and lid-shaped haniwa. Seven lid-shaped haniwa were uncovered across three excavations. === Construction Period === Based on these findings, archaeologist {{ill|Hiroshi Tsude|ja|都出比呂志}}[[ja:都出比呂志]] estimates the mound's construction to around the late 4th century to early 5th century. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Toriimae Kofun (part of the Otokuni Kofun Group) * Designated on March 1, 2016, along with other mounds in the Otokuni Kofun Group, including {{ill|Tenno-no-Mori Kofun|ja|天皇の杜古墳}}[[ja:天皇の杜古墳]], {{ill|Egayama Kofun|ja|恵解山古墳}}[[ja:恵解山古墳]], and {{ill|Terado Otsuka Kofun|ja|寺戸大塚古墳}}[[ja:寺戸大塚古墳]].2016 Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31 == References == {{脚注ヘルプ]] {{Reflist]] == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|last=Tsude|first=Hiroshi|authorlink=Hiroshi Tsude|title=Toriimae Kofun - Comprehensive Report|publisher=Osaka University Archaeological Research Laboratory|year=1990|ref=harv]] * {{Cite book|和書|author=Ōyamazaki Town Board of Education|title=Historic Site Otokuni Kofun Group Toriimae Kofun|url=http://www.town.oyamazaki.kyoto.jp/material/files/group/51/ri-fu.pdf|publisher=Ōyamazaki Town|year=2019|ref=harv]] == External Links == * [http://www.isekiwalker.com/iseki/108736/ Toriimae Kofun] - Iseki Walker β [[Category:Otokuni Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Ōyamazaki Town History]] [[Category:Scallop-shaped Kofun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Kyoto]] [[Nagahouji-Nambahara Kofun]] |{{ill|Torii-mae Kofun|en}}[[ja:鳥居前古墳]] ... 8 KB (1,031 words) - 20:59, [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Toriimae Kofun-qq]] [[Category:Toriimae Kofun]][[Category:kofun stubs]] Scallop-shaped Kofun * {{ill|Torii Mae Kofun|en}}[[ja:鳥居前古墳]]

Seven lid-shaped haniwa were uncovered across three excavations. === Construction Period === Based on these findings, archaeologist {{ill|Hiroshi Tsude|ja|都出比呂志}}[[ja:都出比呂志]] estimates the mound's construction to around the late 4th century to early 5th century. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Toriimae Kofun (part of the Otokuni Kofun Group) * Designated on March 1, 2016, along with other mounds in the Otokuni Kofun Group, including {{ill|Tenno-no-Mori Kofun|ja|天皇の杜古墳|1=Tenno-no-Mori Kofun}}[[ja:天皇の杜古墳]], {{ill|Egayama Kofun|ja|恵解山古墳}}[[ja:恵解山古墳]], and {{ill|Terado Otsuka Kofun|ja|寺戸大塚古墳}}[[ja:寺戸大塚古墳]].2016 Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31 == References == {{脚注ヘルプ]] {{Reflist]] == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|last=Tsude|first=Hiroshi|authorlink=Hiroshi Tsude|title=Toriimae Kofun - Comprehensive Report|publisher=Osaka University Archaeological Research Laboratory|year=1990|ref=harv]] * {{Cite book|和書|author=Ōyamazaki Town Board of Education|title=Historic Site Otokuni Kofun Group Toriimae Kofun|url=http://www.town.oyamazaki.kyoto.jp/material/files/group/51/ri-fu.pdf|publisher=Ōyamazaki Town|year=2019|ref=harv]] == External Links == * [http://www.isekiwalker.com/iseki/108736/ Toriimae Kofun] - Iseki Walker β [[Category:Otokuni Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Ōyamazaki Town History]] [[Category:Scallop-shaped Kofun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Kyoto]] [[Nagahouji-Nambahara Kofun]] |{{ill|Torii-mae Kofun|en}}[[ja:鳥居前古墳]] ... 8 KB (1,031 words) - 20:59, [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Toriimae Kofun-qq]] [[Category:Toriimae Kofun]][[Category:kofun stubs]] Scallop-shaped Kofun * {{ill|Torii Mae Kofun|en}}[[ja:鳥居前古墳]]

revision.comment: fixing links to [[Terado Ōtsuka Kofun]]
revision.text:
{{nihongo|'''Torii-mae Kofun'''|[[:ja:鳥居前古墳|鳥居前古墳]][[ja:鳥居前古墳]]}} is a scallop-shaped burial mound (帆立貝形古墳) located in {{ill|Ōyamazaki Town|ja|大山崎町}}[[ja:大山崎町]], Otokuni District, Kyoto Prefecture. It is designated as a National Historic Site as part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Group|ja|乙訓古墳群}}[[ja:乙訓古墳群]]. The mound was discovered in a 1967 survey and has since undergone three archaeological investigations by Kyoto Prefecture and Osaka University. == Overview == Toriimae Kofun is situated on the northern slopes of Mount Tennozan, known for the famous Battle of Yamazaki, at an elevation of 97 meters. The burial mound was identified during a distribution survey conducted by the Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education in 1967. Excavations of the rear circular section and part of the mound were conducted in 1969. Initially thought to be a keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳), subsequent investigations revised the designation to a scallop-shaped mound. === Burial Facility and Grave Goods === The rear circular section contains a single vertical stone chamber (竪穴式石室), oriented diagonally to the main axis of the mound. Measurements are as follows: * Burial chamber: 9.0 × 4.4 meters * Stone chamber: Length 6.5 meters, width 1.2 meters (west end), 0.77 meters (east end) The chamber's upper stones and ceiling stones were already missing. A clay bed within the chamber likely housed a split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin (割竹形木棺) of 5.3 meters in length. The coffin bed contained: * 2 magatama * 23 cylindrical beads (管玉) * 8 iron swords * 21 daggers * 9 short swords * 2 comma-shaped copper objects (巴形銅器) Additional grave goods, including swords, arrows, armor fragments, and fragments of a deity-beast mirror (画文帯神獣鏡), were found in disturbed soil within the chamber. === Mound Structure === The mound, initially covered in bamboo, had been heavily modified by later activities. Excavations revealed that the mound, originally believed to be keyhole-shaped, is scallop-shaped. It consists of three tiers and measures: * Total length: 51 meters * Diameter of the rear section: 38.4 meters * Length of the frontal section: 12.5 meters * Width of the frontal section: Approximately 31 meters * Height of the rear section: Over 6.5 meters The mound was covered with fukiishi (葺石), sourced locally within 500 meters. The tiers were encircled with cylindrical haniwa, along with jar- and lid-shaped haniwa. Seven lid-shaped haniwa were uncovered across three excavations. === Construction Period === Based on these findings, archaeologist {{ill|Hiroshi Tsude|ja|都出比呂志}}[[ja:都出比呂志]] estimates the mound's construction to around the late 4th century to early 5th century. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Toriimae Kofun (part of the Otokuni Kofun Group) * Designated on March 1, 2016, along with other mounds in the Otokuni Kofun Group, including {{ill|Tenno-no-Mori Kofun|ja|天皇の杜古墳|1=Tenno-no-Mori Kofun}}[[ja:天皇の杜古墳]], {{ill|Egayama Kofun|ja|恵解山古墳}}[[ja:恵解山古墳]], and {{ill|Terado Otsuka Kofun|ja|寺戸大塚古墳|1=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun}}[[ja:寺戸大塚古墳]].2016 Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31 == References == {{脚注ヘルプ]] {{Reflist]] == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|last=Tsude|first=Hiroshi|authorlink=Hiroshi Tsude|title=Toriimae Kofun - Comprehensive Report|publisher=Osaka University Archaeological Research Laboratory|year=1990|ref=harv]] * {{Cite book|和書|author=Ōyamazaki Town Board of Education|title=Historic Site Otokuni Kofun Group Toriimae Kofun|url=http://www.town.oyamazaki.kyoto.jp/material/files/group/51/ri-fu.pdf|publisher=Ōyamazaki Town|year=2019|ref=harv]] == External Links == * [http://www.isekiwalker.com/iseki/108736/ Toriimae Kofun] - Iseki Walker β [[Category:Otokuni Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Ōyamazaki Town History]] [[Category:Scallop-shaped Kofun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Kyoto]] [[Nagahouji-Nambahara Kofun]] |{{ill|Torii-mae Kofun|en}}[[ja:鳥居前古墳]] ... 8 KB (1,031 words) - 20:59, [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Toriimae Kofun-qq]] [[Category:Toriimae Kofun]][[Category:kofun stubs]] Scallop-shaped Kofun * {{ill|Torii Mae Kofun|en}}[[ja:鳥居前古墳]]

The coffin bed contained: * 2 magatama * 23 cylindrical beads (管玉) * 8 iron swords * 21 daggers * 9 short swords * 2 comma-shaped copper objects (巴形銅器) Additional grave goods, including swords, arrows, armor fragments, and fragments of a deity-beast mirror (画文帯神獣鏡), were found in disturbed soil within the chamber. === Mound Structure === The mound, initially covered in bamboo, had been heavily modified by later activities. Excavations revealed that the mound, originally believed to be keyhole-shaped, is scallop-shaped. It consists of three tiers and measures: * Total length: 51 meters * Diameter of the rear section: 38.4 meters * Length of the frontal section: 12.5 meters * Width of the frontal section: Approximately 31 meters * Height of the rear section: Over 6.5 meters The mound was covered with fukiishi (葺石), sourced locally within 500 meters. The tiers were encircled with cylindrical haniwa, along with jar- and lid-shaped haniwa. Seven lid-shaped haniwa were uncovered across three excavations. === Construction Period === Based on these findings, archaeologist {{ill|Hiroshi Tsude|ja|都出比呂志}}[[ja:都出比呂志]] estimates the mound's construction to around the late 4th century to early 5th century. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Toriimae Kofun (part of the Otokuni Kofun Group) * Designated on March 1, 2016, along with other mounds in the Otokuni Kofun Group, including {{ill|Tenno-no-Mori Kofun|ja|天皇の杜古墳|1=Tenno-no-Mori Kofun}}[[ja:天皇の杜古墳]], {{ill|Egayama Kofun|ja|恵解山古墳|1=Igenoyama Kofun}}[[ja:恵解山古墳]], and {{ill|Terado Otsuka Kofun|ja|寺戸大塚古墳|1=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun}}[[ja:寺戸大塚古墳]].2016 Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31 == References == {{脚注ヘルプ]] {{Reflist]] == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|last=Tsude|first=Hiroshi|authorlink=Hiroshi Tsude|title=Toriimae Kofun - Comprehensive Report|publisher=Osaka University Archaeological Research Laboratory|year=1990|ref=harv]] * {{Cite book|和書|author=Ōyamazaki Town Board of Education|title=Historic Site Otokuni Kofun Group Toriimae Kofun|url=http://www.town.oyamazaki.kyoto.jp/material/files/group/51/ri-fu.pdf|publisher=Ōyamazaki Town|year=2019|ref=harv]] == External Links == * [http://www.isekiwalker.com/iseki/108736/ Toriimae Kofun] - Iseki Walker β [[Category:Otokuni Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Ōyamazaki Town History]] [[Category:Scallop-shaped Kofun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Kyoto]] [[Nagahouji-Nambahara Kofun]] |{{ill|Torii-mae Kofun|en}}[[ja:鳥居前古墳]] ... 8 KB (1,031 words) - 20:59, [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Toriimae Kofun-qq]] [[Category:Toriimae Kofun]][[Category:kofun stubs]] Scallop-shaped Kofun * {{ill|Torii Mae Kofun|en}}[[ja:鳥居前古墳]]

It is designated as a National Historic Site as part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Group|ja|乙訓古墳群}}[[ja:乙訓古墳群]]. The mound was discovered in a 1967 survey and has since undergone three archaeological investigations by Kyoto Prefecture and Osaka University. == Overview == Toriimae Kofun is situated on the northern slopes of Mount Tennozan, known for the famous Battle of Yamazaki, at an elevation of 97 meters. Excavations of the rear circular section and part of the mound were conducted in 1969. It is designated as a National Historic Site as part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Group|ja|乙訓古墳群}}[[ja:乙訓古墳群]]. The mound was discovered in a 1967 survey and has since undergone three archaeological investigations by Kyoto Prefecture and Osaka University. == Overview == Toriimae Kofun is situated on the northern slopes of Mount Tennozan, known for the famous Battle of Yamazaki, at an elevation of 97 meters. The burial mound was identified during a distribution survey conducted by the Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education in 1967. Excavations of the rear circular section and part of the mound were conducted in 1969. Initially thought to be a keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳), subsequent investigations revised the designation to a scallop-shaped mound. === Burial Facility and Grave Goods === The rear circular section contains a single vertical stone chamber (竪穴式石室), oriented diagonally to the main axis of the mound. Measurements are as follows: * Burial chamber: 9.0 × 4.4 meters * Stone chamber: Length 6.5 meters, width 1.2 meters (west end), 0.77 meters (east end) The chamber's upper stones and ceiling stones were already missing. A clay bed within the chamber likely housed a split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin (割竹形木棺) of 5.3 meters in length. The coffin bed contained: * 2 magatama * 23 cylindrical beads (管玉) * 8 iron swords * 21 daggers * 9 short swords * 2 comma-shaped copper objects (巴形銅器) Additional grave goods, including swords, arrows, armor fragments, and fragments of a deity-beast mirror (画文帯神獣鏡), were found in disturbed soil within the chamber. === Mound Structure === The mound, initially covered in bamboo, had been heavily modified by later activities. Excavations revealed that the mound, originally believed to be keyhole-shaped, is scallop-shaped. It consists of three tiers and measures: * Total length: 51 meters * Diameter of the rear section: 38.4 meters * Length of the frontal section: 12.5 meters * Width of the frontal section: Approximately 31 meters * Height of the rear section: Over 6.5 meters The mound was covered with fukiishi (葺石), sourced locally within 500 meters. The tiers were encircled with cylindrical haniwa, along with jar- and lid-shaped haniwa. Seven lid-shaped haniwa were uncovered across three excavations. === Construction Period === Based on these findings, archaeologist {{ill|Hiroshi Tsude|ja|都出比呂志}}[[ja:都出比呂志]] estimates the mound's construction to around the late 4th century to early 5th century. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Toriimae Kofun (part of the Otokuni Kofun Group) * Designated on March 1, 2016, along with other mounds in the Otokuni Kofun Group, including {{ill|Tenno-no-Mori Kofun|ja|天皇の杜古墳|1=Tenno-no-Mori Kofun}}[[ja:天皇の杜古墳]], {{ill|Egayama Kofun|ja|恵解山古墳|1=Igenoyama Kofun}}[[ja:恵解山古墳]], and {{ill|Terado Otsuka Kofun|ja|寺戸大塚古墳|1=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun}}[[ja:寺戸大塚古墳]].2016 Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31 == References == {{脚注ヘルプ]] {{Reflist]] == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|last=Tsude|first=Hiroshi|authorlink=Hiroshi Tsude|title=Toriimae Kofun - Comprehensive Report|publisher=Osaka University Archaeological Research Laboratory|year=1990|ref=harv]] * {{Cite book|和書|author=Ōyamazaki Town Board of Education|title=Historic Site Otokuni Kofun Group Toriimae Kofun|url=http://www.town.oyamazaki.kyoto.jp/material/files/group/51/ri-fu.pdf|publisher=Ōyamazaki Town|year=2019|ref=harv]] == External Links == * [http://www.isekiwalker.com/iseki/108736/ Toriimae Kofun] - Iseki Walker β [[Category:Otokuni Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Ōyamazaki Town History]] [[Category:Scallop-shaped Kofun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Kyoto]] [[Nagahouji-Nambahara Kofun]] |{{ill|Torii-mae Kofun|en}}[[ja:鳥居前古墳]] ... 8 KB (1,031 words) - 20:59, [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Toriimae Kofun-qq]] [[Category:Toriimae Kofun]][[Category:kofun stubs]] Scallop-shaped Kofun * {{ill|Torii Mae Kofun|en}}[[ja:鳥居前古墳]]
