{{日本の古墳 |名称=鳥居前古墳 |所属=乙訓古墳群 |image={{画像募集中|cat=乙訓郡}} |所在地=京都府乙訓郡大山崎町円明寺 |形状=帆立貝形古墳 |規模=墳丘長51メートル |築造年代= 4世紀末頃から5世紀初頭 |埋葬施設=竪穴式石室 |被葬者=不明 |出土品=画文帯神獣鏡片・勾玉・巴形銅器 |史跡指定=国の[[史跡]]「鳥居前古墳」
（「乙訓古墳群」に包含）{{Cite web|和書|author=大山崎町生涯学習課文化芸術係|url=https://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000061976.html|title=鳥居前古墳の概要リーフレットはこちらからご覧になれます。|work=大山崎町|date=2019-06-21|accessdate=2022-02-11}} |位置={{coord|34|54|42.5|N|135|40|39.2|E|region:JP-26|display=inline,title}} |地図2={{Location map|Japan Kyoto|lat_deg=34|lat_min=54|lat_sec=42.5|lon_deg=135|lon_min=40|lon_sec=39.2|label=鳥居前古墳|mark=帆立貝形古墳-北北東.svg|marksize=28|border=none|caption=京都府内の位置|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|54|42.5|N|135|40|39.2|E}}}} |特記事項= }} {{nihongo|'''Torii-mae Kofun'''|[[:ja:鳥居前古墳|鳥居前古墳]][[ja:鳥居前古墳]]}} is a {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} (帆立貝形古墳) located in {{ill|Ōyamazaki Town|ja|大山崎町}}[[ja:大山崎町]], Otokuni District, Kyoto Prefecture. It is designated as a National Historic Site as part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Group|ja|乙訓古墳群}}[[ja:乙訓古墳群]]. The mound was discovered in a 1967 survey and has since undergone three archaeological investigations by Kyoto Prefecture and Osaka University. == Overview == Toriimae Kofun is situated on the northern slopes of Mount Tennozan, known for the famous Battle of Yamazaki, at an elevation of 97 meters. The Kofun was identified during a distribution survey conducted by the Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education in 1967. Excavations of the rear circular section and part of the mound were conducted in 1969. Initially thought to be a [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]] (前方後円墳), subsequent investigations revised the designation to a {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}. === Burial Facility and Grave Goods === The rear circular section contains a single {{ill|Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} (竪穴式石室), oriented diagonally to the main axis of the mound. Measurements are as follows: * Burial chamber: 9.0 × 4.4 meters * Stone chamber: Length 6.5 meters, width 1.2 meters (west end), 0.77 meters (east end) The chamber's upper stones and ceiling stones were already missing. A clay bed within the chamber likely housed a split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin (割竹形木棺) of 5.3 meters in length. The coffin bed contained: * 2 [[magatama]] * 23 {{ill|Kudatama|ja|管玉|lt=cylindrical beads}} (管玉) * 8 iron swords * 21 daggers * 9 short swords * 2 comma-shaped copper objects (巴形銅器) Additional grave goods, including swords, arrows, armor fragments, and fragments of a deity-beast mirror (画文帯神獣鏡), were found in disturbed soil within the chamber. === Mound Structure === The mound, initially covered in bamboo, had been heavily modified by later activities. Excavations revealed that the mound, originally believed to be keyhole-shaped, is {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|lt=scallop-shaped}}. It consists of three tiers and measures: * Total length: 51 meters * Diameter of the rear section: 38.4 meters * Length of the frontal section: 12.5 meters * Width of the frontal section: Approximately 31 meters * Height of the rear section: Over 6.5 meters The mound was covered with fukiishi (葺石), sourced locally within 500 meters. The tiers were encircled with cylindrical haniwa, along with jar- and lid-shaped haniwa. Seven lid-shaped haniwa were uncovered across three excavations. === Construction Period === Based on these findings, archaeologist {{ill|Hiroshi Tsude|ja|都出比呂志}}[[ja:都出比呂志]] estimates the mound's construction to around the late 4th century to early 5th century. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Toriimae Kofun (part of the Otokuni Kofun Group) * Designated on March 1, 2016, along with other mounds in the Otokuni Kofun Group, including {{ill|Tenno-no-Mori Kofun|ja|天皇の杜古墳|1=Tenno-no-Mori Kofun}}[[ja:天皇の杜古墳]], {{ill|Egayama Kofun|ja|恵解山古墳|1=Igenoyama Kofun}}[[ja:恵解山古墳]], and {{ill|Terado Otsuka Kofun|ja|寺戸大塚古墳|1=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun}}[[ja:寺戸大塚古墳]].2016 Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31 == References == * == External Links == * [http://www.isekiwalker.com/iseki/108736/ Toriimae Kofun] - Iseki Walker [[Category:Otokuni Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Ōyamazaki Town History]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Kyoto]] [[ja:鳥居前古墳]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{日本の古墳 |名称=鳥居前古墳 |所属=乙訓古墳群 |image={{画像募集中|cat=乙訓郡}} |所在地=京都府乙訓郡大山崎町円明寺 |形状=帆立貝形古墳 |規模=墳丘長51メートル |築造年代= 4世紀末頃から5世紀初頭 |埋葬施設=竪穴式石室 |被葬者=不明 |出土品=画文帯神獣鏡片・勾玉・巴形銅器 |史跡指定=国の[[史跡]]「鳥居前古墳」
（「乙訓古墳群」に包含）{{Cite web|和書|author=大山崎町生涯学習課文化芸術係|url=https://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000061976.html|title=鳥居前古墳の概要リーフレットはこちらからご覧になれます。|work=大山崎町|date=2019-06-21|accessdate=2022-02-11}} |位置={{coord|34|54|42.5|N|135|40|39.2|E|region:JP-26|display=inline,title}} |地図2={{Location map|Japan Kyoto|lat_deg=34|lat_min=54|lat_sec=42.5|lon_deg=135|lon_min=40|lon_sec=39.2|label=鳥居前古墳|mark=帆立貝形古墳-北北東.svg|marksize=28|border=none|caption=京都府内の位置|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|54|42.5|N|135|40|39.2|E}}}} |特記事項= }} {{nihongo|'''Torii-mae Kofun'''|[[:ja:鳥居前古墳|鳥居前古墳]][[ja:鳥居前古墳]]}} is a {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} (帆立貝形古墳) located in {{ill|Ōyamazaki Town|ja|大山崎町}}[[ja:大山崎町]], Otokuni District, Kyoto Prefecture. It is designated as a National Historic Site as part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Group|ja|乙訓古墳群}}[[ja:乙訓古墳群]]. The mound was discovered in a 1967 survey and has since undergone three archaeological investigations by Kyoto Prefecture and Osaka University. == Overview == Toriimae Kofun is situated on the northern slopes of Mount Tennozan, known for the famous Battle of Yamazaki, at an elevation of 97 meters. The Kofun was identified during a distribution survey conducted by the Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education in 1967. Excavations of the rear circular section and part of the mound were conducted in 1969. Initially thought to be a [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]] (前方後円墳), subsequent investigations revised the designation to a {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}. === Burial Facility and Grave Goods === The rear circular section contains a single {{ill|Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} (竪穴式石室), oriented diagonally to the main axis of the mound. Measurements are as follows: * Burial chamber: 9.0 × 4.4 meters * Stone chamber: Length 6.5 meters, width 1.2 meters (west end), 0.77 meters (east end) The chamber's upper stones and ceiling stones were already missing. A clay bed within the chamber likely housed a split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin (割竹形木棺) of 5.3 meters in length. The coffin bed contained: * 2 [[magatama]] * 23 {{ill|Kudatama|ja|管玉|lt=cylindrical beads}} (管玉) * 8 iron swords * 21 daggers * 9 short swords * 2 comma-shaped copper objects (巴形銅器) Additional grave goods, including swords, arrows, armor fragments, and fragments of a deity-beast mirror (画文帯神獣鏡), were found in disturbed soil within the chamber. === Mound Structure === The mound, initially covered in bamboo, had been heavily modified by later activities. Excavations revealed that the mound, originally believed to be keyhole-shaped, is {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|lt=scallop-shaped}}. It consists of three tiers and measures: * Total length: 51 meters * Diameter of the rear section: 38.4 meters * Length of the frontal section: 12.5 meters * Width of the frontal section: Approximately 31 meters * Height of the rear section: Over 6.5 meters The mound was covered with fukiishi (葺石), sourced locally within 500 meters. The tiers were encircled with cylindrical haniwa, along with jar- and lid-shaped haniwa. Seven lid-shaped haniwa were uncovered across three excavations. === Construction Period === Based on these findings, archaeologist {{ill|Hiroshi Tsude|ja|都出比呂志}}[[ja:都出比呂志]] estimates the mound's construction to around the late 4th century to early 5th century. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Toriimae Kofun (part of the Otokuni Kofun Group) * Designated on March 1, 2016, along with other mounds in the Otokuni Kofun Group, including {{ill|Tenno-no-Mori Kofun|ja|天皇の杜古墳|1=Tenno-no-Mori Kofun}}[[ja:天皇の杜古墳]], {{ill|Egayama Kofun|ja|恵解山古墳|1=Igenoyama Kofun}}[[ja:恵解山古墳]], and {{ill|Terado Otsuka Kofun|ja|寺戸大塚古墳|1=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun}}[[ja:寺戸大塚古墳]].2016 Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31 == References == * == External Links == * [http://www.isekiwalker.com/iseki/108736/ Toriimae Kofun] - Iseki Walker [[Category:Otokuni Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Ōyamazaki Town History]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Kyoto]] [[ja:鳥居前古墳]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] revision.comment: Text replacement - "|形状" to "|type"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{日本の古墳 |名称=鳥居前古墳 |所属=乙訓古墳群 |image={{画像募集中|cat=乙訓郡}} |所在地=京都府乙訓郡大山崎町円明寺 |type=帆立貝形古墳 |規模=墳丘長51メートル |築造年代= 4世紀末頃から5世紀初頭 |埋葬施設=竪穴式石室 |被葬者=不明 |burial goods=画文帯神獣鏡片・勾玉・巴形銅器 |史跡指定=国の[[史跡]]「鳥居前古墳」
（「乙訓古墳群」に包含）{{Cite web|和書|author=大山崎町生涯学習課文化芸術係|url=https://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000061976.html|title=鳥居前古墳の概要リーフレットはこちらからご覧になれます。|work=大山崎町|date=2019-06-21|accessdate=2022-02-11}} |位置={{coord|34|54|42.5|N|135|40|39.2|E|region:JP-26|display=inline,title}} |地図2={{Location map|Japan Kyoto|lat_deg=34|lat_min=54|lat_sec=42.5|lon_deg=135|lon_min=40|lon_sec=39.2|label=鳥居前古墳|mark=帆立貝形古墳-北北東.svg|marksize=28|border=none|caption=京都府内の位置|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|54|42.5|N|135|40|39.2|E}}}} |特記事項= }} {{nihongo|'''Torii-mae Kofun'''|[[:ja:鳥居前古墳|鳥居前古墳]][[ja:鳥居前古墳]]}} is a {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} (帆立貝形古墳) located in {{ill|Ōyamazaki Town|ja|大山崎町}}[[ja:大山崎町]], Otokuni District, Kyoto Prefecture. It is designated as a National Historic Site as part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Group|ja|乙訓古墳群}}[[ja:乙訓古墳群]]. The mound was discovered in a 1967 survey and has since undergone three archaeological investigations by Kyoto Prefecture and Osaka University. == Overview == Toriimae Kofun is situated on the northern slopes of Mount Tennozan, known for the famous Battle of Yamazaki, at an elevation of 97 meters. The Kofun was identified during a distribution survey conducted by the Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education in 1967. Excavations of the rear circular section and part of the mound were conducted in 1969. Initially thought to be a [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]] (前方後円墳), subsequent investigations revised the designation to a {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}. === Burial Facility and Grave Goods === The rear circular section contains a single {{ill|Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} (竪穴式石室), oriented diagonally to the main axis of the mound. Measurements are as follows: * Burial chamber: 9.0 × 4.4 meters * Stone chamber: Length 6.5 meters, width 1.2 meters (west end), 0.77 meters (east end) The chamber's upper stones and ceiling stones were already missing. A clay bed within the chamber likely housed a split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin (割竹形木棺) of 5.3 meters in length. The coffin bed contained: * 2 [[magatama]] * 23 {{ill|Kudatama|ja|管玉|lt=cylindrical beads}} (管玉) * 8 iron swords * 21 daggers * 9 short swords * 2 comma-shaped copper objects (巴形銅器) Additional grave goods, including swords, arrows, armor fragments, and fragments of a deity-beast mirror (画文帯神獣鏡), were found in disturbed soil within the chamber. === Mound Structure === The mound, initially covered in bamboo, had been heavily modified by later activities. Excavations revealed that the mound, originally believed to be keyhole-shaped, is {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|lt=scallop-shaped}}. It consists of three tiers and measures: * Total length: 51 meters * Diameter of the rear section: 38.4 meters * Length of the frontal section: 12.5 meters * Width of the frontal section: Approximately 31 meters * Height of the rear section: Over 6.5 meters The mound was covered with fukiishi (葺石), sourced locally within 500 meters. The tiers were encircled with cylindrical haniwa, along with jar- and lid-shaped haniwa. Seven lid-shaped haniwa were uncovered across three excavations. === Construction Period === Based on these findings, archaeologist {{ill|Hiroshi Tsude|ja|都出比呂志}}[[ja:都出比呂志]] estimates the mound's construction to around the late 4th century to early 5th century. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Toriimae Kofun (part of the Otokuni Kofun Group) * Designated on March 1, 2016, along with other mounds in the Otokuni Kofun Group, including {{ill|Tenno-no-Mori Kofun|ja|天皇の杜古墳|1=Tenno-no-Mori Kofun}}[[ja:天皇の杜古墳]], {{ill|Egayama Kofun|ja|恵解山古墳|1=Igenoyama Kofun}}[[ja:恵解山古墳]], and {{ill|Terado Otsuka Kofun|ja|寺戸大塚古墳|1=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun}}[[ja:寺戸大塚古墳]].2016 Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31 == References == * == External Links == * [http://www.isekiwalker.com/iseki/108736/ Toriimae Kofun] - Iseki Walker [[Category:Otokuni Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Ōyamazaki Town History]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Kyoto]] [[ja:鳥居前古墳]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] （「乙訓古墳群」に包含）{{Cite web|和書|author=大山崎町生涯学習課文化芸術係|url=https://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000061976.html|title=鳥居前古墳の概要リーフレットはこちらからご覧になれます。|work=大山崎町|date=2019-06-21|accessdate=2022-02-11}} |位置={{coord|34|54|42.5|N|135|40|39.2|E|region:JP-26|display=inline,title}} |地図2={{Location map|Japan Kyoto|lat_deg=34|lat_min=54|lat_sec=42.5|lon_deg=135|lon_min=40|lon_sec=39.2|label=鳥居前古墳|mark=帆立貝形古墳-北北東.svg|marksize=28|border=none|caption=京都府内の位置|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|54|42.5|N|135|40|39.2|E}}}} |特記事項= }} {{nihongo|'''Torii-mae Kofun'''|[[:ja:鳥居前古墳|鳥居前古墳]][[ja:鳥居前古墳]]}} is a {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} (帆立貝形古墳) located in {{ill|Ōyamazaki Town|ja|大山崎町}}[[ja:大山崎町]], Otokuni District, Kyoto Prefecture. It is designated as a National Historic Site as part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Group|ja|乙訓古墳群}}[[ja:乙訓古墳群]]. The mound was discovered in a 1967 survey and has since undergone three archaeological investigations by Kyoto Prefecture and Osaka University. == Overview == Toriimae Kofun is situated on the northern slopes of Mount Tennozan, known for the famous Battle of Yamazaki, at an elevation of 97 meters. The Kofun was identified during a distribution survey conducted by the Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education in 1967. Excavations of the rear circular section and part of the mound were conducted in 1969. Initially thought to be a [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]] (前方後円墳), subsequent investigations revised the designation to a {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}. === Burial Facility and Grave Goods === The rear circular section contains a single {{ill|Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} (竪穴式石室), oriented diagonally to the main axis of the mound. Measurements are as follows: * Burial chamber: 9.0 × 4.4 meters * Stone chamber: Length 6.5 meters, width 1.2 meters (west end), 0.77 meters (east end) The chamber's upper stones and ceiling stones were already missing. A clay bed within the chamber likely housed a split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin (割竹形木棺) of 5.3 meters in length. The coffin bed contained: * 2 [[magatama]] * 23 {{ill|Kudatama|ja|管玉|lt=cylindrical beads}} (管玉) * 8 iron swords * 21 daggers * 9 short swords * 2 comma-shaped copper objects (巴形銅器) Additional grave goods, including swords, arrows, armor fragments, and fragments of a deity-beast mirror (画文帯神獣鏡), were found in disturbed soil within the chamber. === Mound Structure === The mound, initially covered in bamboo, had been heavily modified by later activities. Excavations revealed that the mound, originally believed to be keyhole-shaped, is {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|lt=scallop-shaped}}. It consists of three tiers and measures: * Total length: 51 meters * Diameter of the rear section: 38.4 meters * Length of the frontal section: 12.5 meters * Width of the frontal section: Approximately 31 meters * Height of the rear section: Over 6.5 meters The mound was covered with fukiishi (葺石), sourced locally within 500 meters. The tiers were encircled with cylindrical haniwa, along with jar- and lid-shaped haniwa. Seven lid-shaped haniwa were uncovered across three excavations. === Construction Period === Based on these findings, archaeologist {{ill|Hiroshi Tsude|ja|都出比呂志}}[[ja:都出比呂志]] estimates the mound's construction to around the late 4th century to early 5th century. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Toriimae Kofun (part of the Otokuni Kofun Group) * Designated on March 1, 2016, along with other mounds in the Otokuni Kofun Group, including {{ill|Tenno-no-Mori Kofun|ja|天皇の杜古墳|1=Tenno-no-Mori Kofun}}[[ja:天皇の杜古墳]], {{ill|Egayama Kofun|ja|恵解山古墳|1=Igenoyama Kofun}}[[ja:恵解山古墳]], and {{ill|Terado Otsuka Kofun|ja|寺戸大塚古墳|1=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun}}[[ja:寺戸大塚古墳]].2016 Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31 == References == * == External Links == * [http://www.isekiwalker.com/iseki/108736/ Toriimae Kofun] - Iseki Walker [[Category:Otokuni Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Ōyamazaki Town History]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Kyoto]] [[ja:鳥居前古墳]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] （「乙訓古墳群」に包含）{{Cite web|和書|author=大山崎町生涯学習課文化芸術係|url=https://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000061976.html|title=鳥居前古墳の概要リーフレットはこちらからご覧になれます。|work=大山崎町|date=2019-06-21|accessdate=2022-02-11}} |位置={{coord|34|54|42.5|N|135|40|39.2|E|region:JP-26|display=inline,title}} |地図2={{Location map|Japan Kyoto|lat_deg=34|lat_min=54|lat_sec=42.5|lon_deg=135|lon_min=40|lon_sec=39.2|label=鳥居前古墳|mark=帆立貝形古墳-北北東.svg|marksize=28|border=none|caption=京都府内の位置|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|54|42.5|N|135|40|39.2|E}}}} |特記事項= }} {{nihongo|'''Torii-mae Kofun'''|[[:ja:鳥居前古墳|鳥居前古墳]][[ja:鳥居前古墳]]}} is a {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} (帆立貝形古墳) located in {{ill|Ōyamazaki Town|ja|大山崎町}}[[ja:大山崎町]], Otokuni District, Kyoto Prefecture. It is designated as a National Historic Site as part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Group|ja|乙訓古墳群}}[[ja:乙訓古墳群]]. The mound was discovered in a 1967 survey and has since undergone three archaeological investigations by Kyoto Prefecture and Osaka University. == Overview == Toriimae Kofun is situated on the northern slopes of Mount Tennozan, known for the famous Battle of Yamazaki, at an elevation of 97 meters. The Kofun was identified during a distribution survey conducted by the Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education in 1967. Excavations of the rear circular section and part of the mound were conducted in 1969. Initially thought to be a [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]] (前方後円墳), subsequent investigations revised the designation to a {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}. === Burial Facility and Grave Goods === The rear circular section contains a single {{ill|Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} (竪穴式石室), oriented diagonally to the main axis of the mound. Measurements are as follows: * Burial chamber: 9.0 × 4.4 meters * Stone chamber: Length 6.5 meters, width 1.2 meters (west end), 0.77 meters (east end) The chamber's upper stones and ceiling stones were already missing. A clay bed within the chamber likely housed a split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin (割竹形木棺) of 5.3 meters in length. The coffin bed contained: * 2 [[magatama]] * 23 {{ill|Kudatama|ja|管玉|lt=cylindrical beads}} (管玉) * 8 iron swords * 21 daggers * 9 short swords * 2 comma-shaped copper objects (巴形銅器) Additional grave goods, including swords, arrows, armor fragments, and fragments of a deity-beast mirror (画文帯神獣鏡), were found in disturbed soil within the chamber. === Mound Structure === The mound, initially covered in bamboo, had been heavily modified by later activities. Excavations revealed that the mound, originally believed to be keyhole-shaped, is {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|lt=scallop-shaped}}. It consists of three tiers and measures: * Total length: 51 meters * Diameter of the rear section: 38.4 meters * Length of the frontal section: 12.5 meters * Width of the frontal section: Approximately 31 meters * Height of the rear section: Over 6.5 meters The mound was covered with fukiishi (葺石), sourced locally within 500 meters. The tiers were encircled with cylindrical haniwa, along with jar- and lid-shaped haniwa. Seven lid-shaped haniwa were uncovered across three excavations. === Construction Period === Based on these findings, archaeologist {{ill|Hiroshi Tsude|ja|都出比呂志}}[[ja:都出比呂志]] estimates the mound's construction to around the late 4th century to early 5th century. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Toriimae Kofun (part of the Otokuni Kofun Group) * Designated on March 1, 2016, along with other mounds in the Otokuni Kofun Group, including {{ill|Tenno-no-Mori Kofun|ja|天皇の杜古墳|1=Tenno-no-Mori Kofun}}[[ja:天皇の杜古墳]], {{ill|Egayama Kofun|ja|恵解山古墳|1=Igenoyama Kofun}}[[ja:恵解山古墳]], and {{ill|Terado Otsuka Kofun|ja|寺戸大塚古墳|1=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun}}[[ja:寺戸大塚古墳]].2016 Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31 == References == * == External Links == * [http://www.isekiwalker.com/iseki/108736/ Toriimae Kofun] - Iseki Walker [[Category:Otokuni Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Ōyamazaki Town History]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Kyoto]] [[ja:鳥居前古墳]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] The Kofun was identified during a distribution survey conducted by the Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education in 1967. Excavations of the rear circular section and part of the mound were conducted in 1969. Initially thought to be a [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]] (前方後円墳), subsequent investigations revised the designation to a {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}. === Burial Facility and Grave Goods === The rear circular section contains a single {{ill|Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} (竪穴式石室), oriented diagonally to the main axis of the mound. Measurements are as follows: * Burial chamber: 9.0 × 4.4 meters * Stone chamber: Length 6.5 meters, width 1.2 meters (west end), 0.77 meters (east end) The chamber's upper stones and ceiling stones were already missing. A clay bed within the chamber likely housed a split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin (割竹形木棺) of 5.3 meters in length. The coffin bed contained: * 2 [[magatama]] * 23 {{ill|Kudatama|ja|管玉|lt=cylindrical beads}} (管玉) * 8 iron swords * 21 daggers * 9 short swords * 2 comma-shaped copper objects (巴形銅器) Additional grave goods, including swords, arrows, armor fragments, and fragments of a deity-beast mirror (画文帯神獣鏡), were found in disturbed soil within the chamber. === Mound Structure === The mound, initially covered in bamboo, had been heavily modified by later activities. Excavations revealed that the mound, originally believed to be keyhole-shaped, is {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|lt=scallop-shaped}}. It consists of three tiers and measures: * Total length: 51 meters * Diameter of the rear section: 38.4 meters * Length of the frontal section: 12.5 meters * Width of the frontal section: Approximately 31 meters * Height of the rear section: Over 6.5 meters The mound was covered with fukiishi (葺石), sourced locally within 500 meters. The tiers were encircled with cylindrical haniwa, along with jar- and lid-shaped haniwa. Seven lid-shaped haniwa were uncovered across three excavations. === built === Based on these findings, archaeologist {{ill|Hiroshi Tsude|ja|都出比呂志}}[[ja:都出比呂志]] estimates the mound's construction to around the late 4th century to early 5th century. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Toriimae Kofun (part of the Otokuni Kofun Group) * Designated on March 1, 2016, along with other mounds in the Otokuni Kofun Group, including {{ill|Tenno-no-Mori Kofun|ja|天皇の杜古墳|1=Tenno-no-Mori Kofun}}[[ja:天皇の杜古墳]], {{ill|Egayama Kofun|ja|恵解山古墳|1=Igenoyama Kofun}}[[ja:恵解山古墳]], and {{ill|Terado Otsuka Kofun|ja|寺戸大塚古墳|1=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun}}[[ja:寺戸大塚古墳]].2016 Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31 == References == It is designated as a National Historic Site as part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Group|ja|乙訓古墳群}}[[ja:乙訓古墳群]]. The mound was discovered in a 1967 survey and has since undergone three archaeological investigations by Kyoto Prefecture and Osaka University. == Overview == Toriimae Kofun is situated on the northern slopes of Mount Tennozan, known for the famous Battle of Yamazaki, at an elevation of 97 meters. The Kofun was identified during a distribution survey conducted by the Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education in 1967. Excavations of the rear circular section and part of the mound were conducted in 1969. Initially thought to be a [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]] (前方後円墳), subsequent investigations revised the designation to a {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}. === Burial Facility and Grave Goods === The rear circular section contains a single {{ill|Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} (竪穴式石室), oriented diagonally to the main axis of the mound. Measurements are as follows: * Burial chamber: 9.0 × 4.4 meters * Stone chamber: Length 6.5 meters, width 1.2 meters (west end), 0.77 meters (east end) The chamber's upper stones and ceiling stones were already missing. A clay bed within the chamber likely housed a split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin (割竹形木棺) of 5.3 meters in length. The coffin bed contained: * 2 [[magatama]] * 23 {{ill|Kudatama|ja|管玉|lt=cylindrical beads}} (管玉) * 8 iron swords * 21 daggers * 9 short swords * 2 comma-shaped copper objects (巴形銅器) Additional grave goods, including swords, arrows, armor fragments, and fragments of a deity-beast mirror (画文帯神獣鏡), were found in disturbed soil within the chamber. === Mound Structure === The mound, initially covered in bamboo, had been heavily modified by later activities. Excavations revealed that the mound, originally believed to be keyhole-shaped, is {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|lt=scallop-shaped}}. It consists of three tiers and measures: * Total length: 51 meters * Diameter of the rear section: 38.4 meters * Length of the frontal section: 12.5 meters * Width of the frontal section: Approximately 31 meters * Height of the rear section: Over 6.5 meters The mound was covered with fukiishi (葺石), sourced locally within 500 meters. The tiers were encircled with cylindrical haniwa, along with jar- and lid-shaped haniwa. Seven lid-shaped haniwa were uncovered across three excavations. === built === Based on these findings, archaeologist {{ill|Hiroshi Tsude|ja|都出比呂志}}[[ja:都出比呂志]] estimates the mound's construction to around the late 4th century to early 5th century. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Toriimae Kofun (part of the Otokuni Kofun Group) * Designated on March 1, 2016, along with other mounds in the Otokuni Kofun Group, including {{ill|Tenno-no-Mori Kofun|ja|天皇の杜古墳|1=Tenno-no-Mori Kofun}}[[ja:天皇の杜古墳]], {{ill|Egayama Kofun|ja|恵解山古墳|1=Igenoyama Kofun}}[[ja:恵解山古墳]], and The tiers were encircled with cylindrical haniwa, along with jar- and lid-shaped haniwa. Seven lid-shaped haniwa were uncovered across three excavations. === built === Based on these findings, archaeologist {{ill|Hiroshi Tsude|ja|都出比呂志}}[[ja:都出比呂志]] estimates the mound's construction to around the late 4th century to early 5th century. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Toriimae Kofun (part of the Otokuni Kofun part_of) * Designated on March 1, 2016, along with other mounds in the Otokuni Kofun part_of, including {{ill|Tenno-no-Mori Kofun|ja|天皇の杜古墳|1=Tenno-no-Mori Kofun}}[[ja:天皇の杜古墳]], {{ill|Egayama Kofun|ja|恵解山古墳|1=Igenoyama Kofun}}[[ja:恵解山古墳]], and {{ill|Terado Otsuka Kofun|ja|寺戸大塚古墳|1=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun}}[[ja:寺戸大塚古墳]].2016 Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31 == References == revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{日本の古墳 |名称=鳥居前古墳 |所属=乙訓古墳群 |image={{画像募集中|cat=乙訓郡}} |所在地=京都府乙訓郡大山崎町円明寺 |type=帆立貝形古墳 |規模=墳丘長51メートル |築造年代= 4世紀末頃から5世紀初頭 |埋葬施設=竪穴式石室 |被葬者=不明 |burial goods=画文帯神獣鏡片・勾玉・巴形銅器 |史跡指定=国の[[史跡]]「鳥居前古墳」
（「乙訓古墳群」に包含）{{Cite web|和書|author=大山崎町生涯学習課文化芸術係|url=https://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000061976.html|title=鳥居前古墳の概要リーフレットはこちらからご覧になれます。|work=大山崎町|date=2019-06-21|accessdate=2022-02-11}} |位置={{coord|34|54|42.5|N|135|40|39.2|E|region:JP-26|display=inline,title}} |地図2={{Location map|Japan Kyoto|lat_deg=34|lat_min=54|lat_sec=42.5|lon_deg=135|lon_min=40|lon_sec=39.2|label=鳥居前古墳|mark=帆立貝形古墳-北北東.svg|marksize=28|border=none|caption=京都府内の位置|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|54|42.5|N|135|40|39.2|E}}}} |特記事項= }} {{nihongo|'''Torii-mae Kofun'''|[[:ja:鳥居前古墳|鳥居前古墳]][[ja:鳥居前古墳]]}} is a {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} (帆立貝形古墳) located in {{ill|Ōyamazaki Town|ja|大山崎町}}[[ja:大山崎町]], Otokuni District, Kyoto Prefecture. It is designated as a National Historic Site as part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Group|ja|乙訓古墳群}}[[ja:乙訓古墳群]]. The mound was discovered in a 1967 survey and has since undergone three archaeological investigations by Kyoto Prefecture and Osaka University. == Overview == Toriimae Kofun is situated on the northern slopes of Mount Tennozan, known for the famous Battle of Yamazaki, at an elevation of 97 meters. The Kofun was identified during a distribution survey conducted by the Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education in 1967. Excavations of the rear circular section and part of the mound were conducted in 1969. Initially thought to be a [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]] (前方後円墳), subsequent investigations revised the designation to a {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}. === Burial Facility and Grave Goods === The rear circular section contains a single {{ill|Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} (竪穴式石室), oriented diagonally to the main axis of the mound. Measurements are as follows: * Burial chamber: 9.0 × 4.4 meters * Stone chamber: Length 6.5 meters, width 1.2 meters (west end), 0.77 meters (east end) The chamber's upper stones and ceiling stones were already missing. A clay bed within the chamber likely housed a split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin (割竹形木棺) of 5.3 meters in length. The coffin bed contained: * 2 [[magatama]] * 23 {{ill|Kudatama|ja|管玉|lt=cylindrical beads}} (管玉) * 8 iron swords * 21 daggers * 9 short swords * 2 comma-shaped copper objects (巴形銅器) Additional grave goods, including swords, arrows, armor fragments, and fragments of a deity-beast mirror (画文帯神獣鏡), were found in disturbed soil within the chamber. === Mound Structure === The mound, initially covered in bamboo, had been heavily modified by later activities. Excavations revealed that the mound, originally believed to be keyhole-shaped, is {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|lt=scallop-shaped}}. It consists of three tiers and measures: * Total length: 51 meters * Diameter of the rear section: 38.4 meters * Length of the frontal section: 12.5 meters * Width of the frontal section: Approximately 31 meters * Height of the rear section: Over 6.5 meters The mound was covered with fukiishi (葺石), sourced locally within 500 meters. The tiers were encircled with cylindrical haniwa, along with jar- and lid-shaped haniwa. Seven lid-shaped haniwa were uncovered across three excavations. === built === Based on these findings, archaeologist {{ill|Hiroshi Tsude|ja|都出比呂志}}[[ja:都出比呂志]] estimates the mound's construction to around the late 4th century to early 5th century. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Toriimae Kofun (part of the Otokuni Kofun Group) * Designated on March 1, 2016, along with other mounds in the Otokuni Kofun Group, including {{ill|Tenno-no-Mori Kofun|ja|天皇の杜古墳|1=Tenno-no-Mori Kofun}}[[ja:天皇の杜古墳]], {{ill|Egayama Kofun|ja|恵解山古墳|1=Igenoyama Kofun}}[[ja:恵解山古墳]], and {{ill|Terado Otsuka Kofun|ja|寺戸大塚古墳|1=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun}}[[ja:寺戸大塚古墳]].2016 Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31 == References == This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{日本の古墳 |名称=鳥居前古墳 |所属=乙訓古墳群 |image={{画像募集中|cat=乙訓郡}} |所在地=京都府乙訓郡大山崎町円明寺 |type=帆立貝形古墳 |規模=墳丘長51メートル |築造年代= 4世紀末頃から5世紀初頭 |埋葬施設=竪穴式石室 |被葬者=不明 |burial goods=画文帯神獣鏡片・勾玉・巴形銅器 |史跡指定=国の[[史跡]]「鳥居前古墳」
（「乙訓古墳群」に包含）{{Cite web|和書|author=大山崎町生涯学習課文化芸術係|url=https://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000061976.html|title=鳥居前古墳の概要リーフレットはこちらからご覧になれます。|work=大山崎町|date=2019-06-21|accessdate=2022-02-11}} |位置={{coord|34|54|42.5|N|135|40|39.2|E|region:JP-26|display=inline,title}} |地図2={{Location map|Japan Kyoto|lat_deg=34|lat_min=54|lat_sec=42.5|lon_deg=135|lon_min=40|lon_sec=39.2|label=鳥居前古墳|mark=帆立貝形古墳-北北東.svg|marksize=28|border=none|caption=京都府内の位置|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|54|42.5|N|135|40|39.2|E}}}} |特記事項= }} {{nihongo|'''Torii-mae Kofun'''|[[:ja:鳥居前古墳|鳥居前古墳]][[ja:鳥居前古墳]]}} is a {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} (帆立貝形古墳) located in {{ill|Ōyamazaki Town|ja|大山崎町}}[[ja:大山崎町]], Otokuni District, Kyoto Prefecture. It is designated as a National Historic Site as part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Group|ja|乙訓古墳群}}[[ja:乙訓古墳群]]. The mound was discovered in a 1967 survey and has since undergone three archaeological investigations by Kyoto Prefecture and Osaka University. == Overview == Toriimae Kofun is situated on the northern slopes of Mount Tennozan, known for the famous Battle of Yamazaki, at an elevation of 97 meters. The Kofun was identified during a distribution survey conducted by the Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education in 1967. Excavations of the rear circular section and part of the mound were conducted in 1969. Initially thought to be a [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]] (前方後円墳), subsequent investigations revised the designation to a {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}. === Burial Facility and Grave Goods === The rear circular section contains a single {{ill|Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} (竪穴式石室), oriented diagonally to the main axis of the mound. Measurements are as follows: * Burial chamber: 9.0 × 4.4 meters * Stone chamber: Length 6.5 meters, width 1.2 meters (west end), 0.77 meters (east end) The chamber's upper stones and ceiling stones were already missing. A clay bed within the chamber likely housed a split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin (割竹形木棺) of 5.3 meters in length. The coffin bed contained: * 2 [[magatama]] * 23 {{ill|Kudatama|ja|管玉|lt=cylindrical beads}} (管玉) * 8 iron swords * 21 daggers * 9 short swords * 2 comma-shaped copper objects (巴形銅器) Additional grave goods, including swords, arrows, armor fragments, and fragments of a deity-beast mirror (画文帯神獣鏡), were found in disturbed soil within the chamber. === Mound Structure === The mound, initially covered in bamboo, had been heavily modified by later activities. Excavations revealed that the mound, originally believed to be keyhole-shaped, is {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|lt=scallop-shaped}}. It consists of three tiers and measures: * Total length: 51 meters * Diameter of the rear section: 38.4 meters * Length of the frontal section: 12.5 meters * Width of the frontal section: Approximately 31 meters * Height of the rear section: Over 6.5 meters The mound was covered with fukiishi (葺石), sourced locally within 500 meters. The tiers were encircled with cylindrical haniwa, along with jar- and lid-shaped haniwa. Seven lid-shaped haniwa were uncovered across three excavations. === built === Based on these findings, archaeologist {{ill|Hiroshi Tsude|ja|都出比呂志}}[[ja:都出比呂志]] estimates the mound's construction to around the late 4th century to early 5th century. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Toriimae Kofun (part of the Otokuni Kofun Group) * Designated on March 1, 2016, along with other mounds in the Otokuni Kofun Group, including {{ill|Tenno-no-Mori Kofun|ja|天皇の杜古墳|1=Tenno-no-Mori Kofun}}[[ja:天皇の杜古墳]], {{ill|Egayama Kofun|ja|恵解山古墳|1=Igenoyama Kofun}}[[ja:恵解山古墳]], and {{ill|Terado Otsuka Kofun|ja|寺戸大塚古墳|1=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun}}[[ja:寺戸大塚古墳]].2016 Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31 == References == revision.comment: trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{日本の古墳 |名称=鳥居前古墳 |所属=乙訓古墳群 |image={{画像募集中|cat=乙訓郡}} |所在地=京都府乙訓郡大山崎町円明寺 |type=帆立貝形古墳 |規模=墳丘長51メートル |築造年代= 4世紀末頃から5世紀初頭 |埋葬施設=竪穴式石室 |被葬者=不明 |burial goods=画文帯神獣鏡片・勾玉・巴形銅器 |史跡指定=国の[[史跡]]「鳥居前古墳」
（「乙訓古墳群」に包含）{{Cite web|和書|author=大山崎町生涯学習課文化芸術係|url=https://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000061976.html|title=鳥居前古墳の概要リーフレットはこちらからご覧になれます。|work=大山崎町|date=2019-06-21|accessdate=2022-02-11}} |位置={{coord|34|54|42.5|N|135|40|39.2|E|region:JP-26|display=inline,title}} |地図2={{Location map|Japan Kyoto|lat_deg=34|lat_min=54|lat_sec=42.5|lon_deg=135|lon_min=40|lon_sec=39.2|label=鳥居前古墳|mark=帆立貝形古墳-北北東.svg|marksize=28|border=none|caption=京都府内の位置|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|54|42.5|N|135|40|39.2|E}}}} |特記事項= }} {{nihongo|'''Torii-mae Kofun'''|[[:ja:鳥居前古墳|鳥居前古墳]][[ja:鳥居前古墳]]}} is a {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} (帆立貝形古墳) located in {{ill|Ōyamazaki Town|ja|大山崎町}}[[ja:大山崎町]], Otokuni District, Kyoto Prefecture. It is designated as a National Historic Site as part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Group|ja|乙訓古墳群}}[[ja:乙訓古墳群]]. The mound was discovered in a 1967 survey and has since undergone three archaeological investigations by Kyoto Prefecture and Osaka University. == Overview == Toriimae Kofun is situated on the northern slopes of Mount Tennozan, known for the famous Battle of Yamazaki, at an elevation of 97 meters. The Kofun was identified during a distribution survey conducted by the Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education in 1967. Excavations of the rear circular section and part of the mound were conducted in 1969. Initially thought to be a [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]] (前方後円墳), subsequent investigations revised the designation to a {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}. === burial and Grave Goods === The rear circular section contains a single {{ill|Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} (竪穴式石室), oriented diagonally to the main axis of the mound. Measurements are as follows: * Burial chamber: 9.0 × 4.4 meters * Stone chamber: Length 6.5 meters, width 1.2 meters (west end), 0.77 meters (east end) The chamber's upper stones and ceiling stones were already missing. A clay bed within the chamber likely housed a split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin (割竹形木棺) of 5.3 meters in length. The coffin bed contained: * 2 [[magatama]] * 23 {{ill|Kudatama|ja|管玉|lt=cylindrical beads}} (管玉) * 8 iron swords * 21 daggers * 9 short swords * 2 comma-shaped copper objects (巴形銅器) Additional grave goods, including swords, arrows, armor fragments, and fragments of a deity-beast mirror (画文帯神獣鏡), were found in disturbed soil within the chamber. === Mound Structure === The mound, initially covered in bamboo, had been heavily modified by later activities. Excavations revealed that the mound, originally believed to be keyhole-shaped, is {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|lt=scallop-shaped}}. It consists of three tiers and measures: * Total length: 51 meters * Diameter of the rear section: 38.4 meters * Length of the frontal section: 12.5 meters * Width of the frontal section: Approximately 31 meters * Height of the rear section: Over 6.5 meters The mound was covered with fukiishi (葺石), sourced locally within 500 meters. The tiers were encircled with cylindrical haniwa, along with jar- and lid-shaped haniwa. Seven lid-shaped haniwa were uncovered across three excavations. === built === Based on these findings, archaeologist {{ill|Hiroshi Tsude|ja|都出比呂志}}[[ja:都出比呂志]] estimates the mound's construction to around the late 4th century to early 5th century. == other_designation == === National Historic Site === * Toriimae Kofun (part of the Otokuni Kofun Group) * Designated on March 1, 2016, along with other mounds in the Otokuni Kofun Group, including {{ill|Tenno-no-Mori Kofun|ja|天皇の杜古墳|1=Tenno-no-Mori Kofun}}[[ja:天皇の杜古墳]], {{ill|Egayama Kofun|ja|恵解山古墳|1=Igenoyama Kofun}}[[ja:恵解山古墳]], and {{ill|Terado Otsuka Kofun|ja|寺戸大塚古墳|1=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun}}[[ja:寺戸大塚古墳]].2016 Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31 == References == {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{日本の古墳 |名称=鳥居前古墳 |所属=乙訓古墳群 |image={{画像募集中|cat=乙訓郡}} |所在地=京都府乙訓郡大山崎町円明寺 |type=帆立貝形古墳 |規模=墳丘長51メートル |築造年代= 4世紀末頃から5世紀初頭 |埋葬施設=竪穴式石室 |被葬者=不明 |burial goods=画文帯神獣鏡片・勾玉・巴形銅器 |史跡指定=国の[[史跡]]「鳥居前古墳」
（「乙訓古墳群」に包含）{{Cite web|和書|author=大山崎町生涯学習課文化芸術係|url=https://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000061976.html|title=鳥居前古墳の概要リーフレットはこちらからご覧になれます。|work=大山崎町|date=2019-06-21|accessdate=2022-02-11}} |位置={{coord|34|54|42.5|N|135|40|39.2|E|region:JP-26|display=inline,title}} |地図2={{Location map|Japan Kyoto|lat_deg=34|lat_min=54|lat_sec=42.5|lon_deg=135|lon_min=40|lon_sec=39.2|label=鳥居前古墳|mark=帆立貝形古墳-北北東.svg|marksize=28|border=none|caption=京都府内の位置|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|54|42.5|N|135|40|39.2|E}}}} |特記事項= }} {{nihongo|'''Torii-mae Kofun'''|[[:ja:鳥居前古墳|鳥居前古墳]][[ja:鳥居前古墳]]}} is a {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} (帆立貝形古墳) located in {{ill|Ōyamazaki Town|ja|大山崎町}}[[ja:大山崎町]], Otokuni District, Kyoto Prefecture. It is designated as a National Historic Site as part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Group|ja|乙訓古墳群}}[[ja:乙訓古墳群]]. The mound was discovered in a 1967 survey and has since undergone three archaeological investigations by Kyoto Prefecture and Osaka University. == Overview == Toriimae Kofun is situated on the northern slopes of Mount Tennozan, known for the famous Battle of Yamazaki, at an elevation of 97 meters. The Kofun was identified during a distribution survey conducted by the Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education in 1967. Excavations of the rear circular section and part of the mound were conducted in 1969. Initially thought to be a [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]] (前方後円墳), subsequent investigations revised the designation to a {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}. === burial and Grave Goods === The rear circular section contains a single {{ill|Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} (竪穴式石室), oriented diagonally to the main axis of the mound. Measurements are as follows: * Burial chamber: 9.0 × 4.4 meters * Stone chamber: Length 6.5 meters, width 1.2 meters (west end), 0.77 meters (east end) The chamber's upper stones and ceiling stones were already missing. A clay bed within the chamber likely housed a split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin (割竹形木棺) of 5.3 meters in length. The coffin bed contained: * 2 [[magatama]] * 23 {{ill|Kudatama|ja|管玉|lt=cylindrical beads}} (管玉) * 8 iron swords * 21 daggers * 9 short swords * 2 comma-shaped copper objects (巴形銅器) Additional grave goods, including swords, arrows, armor fragments, and fragments of a deity-beast mirror (画文帯神獣鏡), were found in disturbed soil within the chamber. === Mound Structure === The mound, initially covered in bamboo, had been heavily modified by later activities. Excavations revealed that the mound, originally believed to be keyhole-shaped, is {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|lt=scallop-shaped}}. It consists of three tiers and measures: * Total length: 51 meters * Diameter of the rear section: 38.4 meters * Length of the frontal section: 12.5 meters * Width of the frontal section: Approximately 31 meters * Height of the rear section: Over 6.5 meters The mound was covered with fukiishi (葺石), sourced locally within 500 meters. The tiers were encircled with cylindrical haniwa, along with jar- and lid-shaped haniwa. Seven lid-shaped haniwa were uncovered across three excavations. === built === Based on these findings, archaeologist {{ill|Hiroshi Tsude|ja|都出比呂志}}[[ja:都出比呂志]] estimates the mound's construction to around the late 4th century to early 5th century. == other_designation == === National Historic Site === * Toriimae Kofun (part of the Otokuni Kofun Group) * Designated on March 1, 2016, along with other mounds in the Otokuni Kofun Group, including {{ill|Tenno-no-Mori Kofun|ja|天皇の杜古墳|1=Tenno-no-Mori Kofun}}[[ja:天皇の杜古墳]], {{ill|Egayama Kofun|ja|恵解山古墳|1=Igenoyama Kofun}}[[ja:恵解山古墳]], and {{ill|Terado Otsuka Kofun|ja|寺戸大塚古墳|1=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun}}[[ja:寺戸大塚古墳]].2016 Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31 == References == {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{日本の古墳 |名称=鳥居前古墳 |所属=乙訓古墳群 |image={{画像募集中|cat=乙訓郡}} |所在地=京都府乙訓郡大山崎町円明寺 |type=帆立貝形古墳 |規模=墳丘長51メートル |築造年代= 4世紀末頃から5世紀初頭 |埋葬施設=竪穴式石室 |被葬者=不明 |burial goods=画文帯神獣鏡片・勾玉・巴形銅器 |史跡指定=国の[[史跡]]「鳥居前古墳」
（「乙訓古墳群」に包含）{{Cite web|和書|author=大山崎町生涯学習課文化芸術係|url=https://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000061976.html|title=鳥居前古墳の概要リーフレットはこちらからご覧になれます。|work=大山崎町|date=2019-06-21|accessdate=2022-02-11}} |位置={{coord|34|54|42.5|N|135|40|39.2|E|region:JP-26|display=inline,title}} |地図2={{Location map|Japan Kyoto|lat_deg=34|lat_min=54|lat_sec=42.5|lon_deg=135|lon_min=40|lon_sec=39.2|label=鳥居前古墳|mark=帆立貝形古墳-北北東.svg|marksize=28|border=none|caption=京都府内の位置|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|54|42.5|N|135|40|39.2|E}}}} |特記事項= }} {{nihongo|'''Torii-mae Kofun'''|[[:ja:鳥居前古墳|鳥居前古墳]][[ja:鳥居前古墳]]}} is a {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} (帆立貝形古墳) located in {{ill|Ōyamazaki Town|ja|大山崎町}}[[ja:大山崎町]], Otokuni District, Kyoto Prefecture. It is designated as a National Historic Site as part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Group|ja|乙訓古墳群}}[[ja:乙訓古墳群]]. The mound was discovered in a 1967 survey and has since undergone three archaeological investigations by Kyoto Prefecture and Osaka University. == Overview == Toriimae Kofun is situated on the northern slopes of Mount Tennozan, known for the famous Battle of Yamazaki, at an elevation of 97 meters. The Kofun was identified during a distribution survey conducted by the Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education in 1967. Excavations of the rear circular section and part of the mound were conducted in 1969. Initially thought to be a [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]] (前方後円墳), subsequent investigations revised the designation to a {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}. === burial and Grave Goods === The rear circular section contains a single {{ill|Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} (竪穴式石室), oriented diagonally to the main axis of the mound. Measurements are as follows: * Burial chamber: 9.0 × 4.4 meters * Stone chamber: Length 6.5 meters, width 1.2 meters (west end), 0.77 meters (east end) The chamber's upper stones and ceiling stones were already missing. A clay bed within the chamber likely housed a split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin (割竹形木棺) of 5.3 meters in length. The coffin bed contained: * 2 [[magatama]] * 23 {{ill|Kudatama|ja|管玉|lt=cylindrical beads}} (管玉) * 8 iron swords * 21 daggers * 9 short swords * 2 comma-shaped copper objects (巴形銅器) Additional grave goods, including swords, arrows, armor fragments, and fragments of a deity-beast mirror (画文帯神獣鏡), were found in disturbed soil within the chamber. === Mound Structure === The mound, initially covered in bamboo, had been heavily modified by later activities. Excavations revealed that the mound, originally believed to be keyhole-shaped, is {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|lt=scallop-shaped}}. It consists of three tiers and measures: * Total length: 51 meters * Diameter of the rear section: 38.4 meters * Length of the frontal section: 12.5 meters * Width of the frontal section: Approximately 31 meters * Height of the rear section: Over 6.5 meters The mound was covered with fukiishi (葺石), sourced locally within 500 meters. The tiers were encircled with cylindrical haniwa, along with jar- and lid-shaped haniwa. Seven lid-shaped haniwa were uncovered across three excavations. === built === Based on these findings, archaeologist {{ill|Hiroshi Tsude|ja|都出比呂志}}[[ja:都出比呂志]] estimates the mound's construction to around the late 4th century to early 5th century. == other_designation == === National Historic Site === * Toriimae Kofun (part of the Otokuni Kofun Group) * Designated on March 1, 2016, along with other mounds in the Otokuni Kofun Group, including {{ill|Tenno-no-Mori Kofun|ja|天皇の杜古墳|1=Tenno-no-Mori Kofun}}[[ja:天皇の杜古墳]], {{ill|Egayama Kofun|ja|恵解山古墳|1=Igenoyama Kofun}}[[ja:恵解山古墳]], and {{ill|Terado Otsuka Kofun|ja|寺戸大塚古墳|1=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun}}[[ja:寺戸大塚古墳]].2016 Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31 == References == {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{日本の古墳 |名称=鳥居前古墳 |所属=乙訓古墳群 |image={{画像募集中|cat=乙訓郡}} |所在地=京都府乙訓郡大山崎町円明寺 |type=帆立貝形古墳 |規模=墳丘長51メートル |築造年代= 4世紀末頃から5世紀初頭 |埋葬施設=竪穴式石室 |被葬者=不明 |burial goods=画文帯神獣鏡片・勾玉・巴形銅器 |史跡指定=国の[[史跡]]「鳥居前古墳」
（「乙訓古墳群」に包含）{{Cite web|和書|author=大山崎町生涯学習課文化芸術係|url=https://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000061976.html|title=鳥居前古墳の概要リーフレットはこちらからご覧になれます。|work=大山崎町|date=2019-06-21|accessdate=2022-02-11}} |位置={{coord|34|54|42.5|N|135|40|39.2|E|region:JP-26|display=inline,title}} |地図2={{location map|Japan Kyoto|lat_deg=34|lat_min=54|lat_sec=42.5|lon_deg=135|lon_min=40|lon_sec=39.2|label=鳥居前古墳|mark=帆立貝形古墳-北北東.svg|marksize=28|border=none|caption=京都府内の位置|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|54|42.5|N|135|40|39.2|E}}}} |特記事項= }} {{nihongo|'''Torii-mae Kofun'''|[[:ja:鳥居前古墳|鳥居前古墳]][[ja:鳥居前古墳]]}} is a {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} (帆立貝形古墳) located in {{ill|Ōyamazaki Town|ja|大山崎町}}[[ja:大山崎町]], Otokuni District, Kyoto Prefecture. It is designated as a National other_designation as part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Group|ja|乙訓古墳群}}[[ja:乙訓古墳群]]. The mound was discovered in a 1967 survey and has since undergone three archaeological investigations by Kyoto Prefecture and Osaka University. == Overview == Toriimae Kofun is situated on the northern slopes of Mount Tennozan, known for the famous Battle of Yamazaki, at an elevation of 97 meters. The Kofun was identified during a distribution survey conducted by the Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education in 1967. Excavations of the rear circular section and part of the mound were conducted in 1969. Initially thought to be a [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]] (前方後円墳), subsequent investigations revised the designation to a {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}. === burial and Grave Goods === The rear circular section contains a single {{ill|Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} (竪穴式石室), oriented diagonally to the main axis of the mound. Measurements are as follows: * Burial chamber: 9.0 × 4.4 meters * Stone chamber: Length 6.5 meters, width 1.2 meters (west end), 0.77 meters (east end) The chamber's upper stones and ceiling stones were already missing. A clay bed within the chamber likely housed a split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin (割竹形木棺) of 5.3 meters in length. The coffin bed contained: * 2 [[magatama]] * 23 {{ill|Kudatama|ja|管玉|lt=cylindrical beads}} (管玉) * 8 iron swords * 21 daggers * 9 short swords * 2 comma-shaped copper objects (巴形銅器) Additional grave goods, including swords, arrows, armor fragments, and fragments of a deity-beast mirror (画文帯神獣鏡), were found in disturbed soil within the chamber. === Mound Structure === The mound, initially covered in bamboo, had been heavily modified by later activities. Excavations revealed that the mound, originally believed to be keyhole-shaped, is {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|lt=scallop-shaped}}. It consists of three tiers and measures: * Total length: 51 meters * Diameter of the rear section: 38.4 meters * Length of the frontal section: 12.5 meters * Width of the frontal section: Approximately 31 meters * Height of the rear section: Over 6.5 meters The mound was covered with fukiishi (葺石), sourced locally within 500 meters. The tiers were encircled with cylindrical haniwa, along with jar- and lid-shaped haniwa. Seven lid-shaped haniwa were uncovered across three excavations. === built === Based on these findings, archaeologist {{ill|Hiroshi Tsude|ja|都出比呂志}}[[ja:都出比呂志]] estimates the mound's construction to around the late 4th century to early 5th century. == other_designation == === National other_designation === * Toriimae Kofun (part of the Otokuni Kofun Group) * Designated on March 1, 2016, along with other mounds in the Otokuni Kofun Group, including {{ill|Tenno-no-Mori Kofun|ja|天皇の杜古墳|1=Tenno-no-Mori Kofun}}[[ja:天皇の杜古墳]], {{ill|Egayama Kofun|ja|恵解山古墳|1=Igenoyama Kofun}}[[ja:恵解山古墳]], and {{ill|Terado Otsuka Kofun|ja|寺戸大塚古墳|1=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun}}[[ja:寺戸大塚古墳]].2016 Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31 == References == {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{日本の古墳 |名称=鳥居前古墳 |所属=乙訓古墳群 |image={{画像募集中|cat=乙訓郡}} |location=京都府乙訓郡大山崎町円明寺 |type=帆立貝形古墳 |area=墳丘長51メートル |築造年代= 4世紀末頃から5世紀初頭 |埋葬施設=竪穴式石室 |被葬者=不明 |burial goods=画文帯神獣鏡片・勾玉・巴形銅器 |史跡指定=国の[[史跡]]「鳥居前古墳」
（「乙訓古墳群」に包含）{{Cite web|和書|author=大山崎町生涯学習課文化芸術係|url=https://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000061976.html|title=鳥居前古墳の概要リーフレットはこちらからご覧になれます。|work=大山崎町|date=2019-06-21|accessdate=2022-02-11}} |位置={{coord|34|54|42.5|N|135|40|39.2|E|region:JP-26|display=inline,title}} |地図2={{location map|Japan Kyoto|lat_deg=34|lat_min=54|lat_sec=42.5|lon_deg=135|lon_min=40|lon_sec=39.2|label=鳥居前古墳|mark=帆立貝形古墳-北北東.svg|marksize=28|border=none|caption=京都府内の位置|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|54|42.5|N|135|40|39.2|E}}}} |特記事項= }} {{nihongo|'''Torii-mae Kofun'''|[[:ja:鳥居前古墳|鳥居前古墳]][[ja:鳥居前古墳]]}} is a {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} (帆立貝形古墳) located in {{ill|Ōyamazaki Town|ja|大山崎町}}[[ja:大山崎町]], Otokuni District, Kyoto Prefecture. It is designated as a National other_designation as part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Group|ja|乙訓古墳群}}[[ja:乙訓古墳群]]. The mound was discovered in a 1967 survey and has since undergone three archaeological investigations by Kyoto Prefecture and Osaka University. == Overview == Toriimae Kofun is situated on the northern slopes of Mount Tennozan, known for the famous Battle of Yamazaki, at an elevation of 97 meters. The Kofun was identified during a distribution survey conducted by the Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education in 1967. Excavations of the rear circular section and part of the mound were conducted in 1969. Initially thought to be a [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]] (前方後円墳), subsequent investigations revised the designation to a {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}. === burial and Grave Goods === The rear circular section contains a single {{ill|Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} (竪穴式石室), oriented diagonally to the main axis of the mound. Measurements are as follows: * Burial chamber: 9.0 × 4.4 meters * Stone chamber: Length 6.5 meters, width 1.2 meters (west end), 0.77 meters (east end) The chamber's upper stones and ceiling stones were already missing. A clay bed within the chamber likely housed a split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin (割竹形木棺) of 5.3 meters in length. The coffin bed contained: * 2 [[magatama]] * 23 {{ill|Kudatama|ja|管玉|lt=cylindrical beads}} (管玉) * 8 iron swords * 21 daggers * 9 short swords * 2 comma-shaped copper objects (巴形銅器) Additional grave goods, including swords, arrows, armor fragments, and fragments of a deity-beast mirror (画文帯神獣鏡), were found in disturbed soil within the chamber. === Mound Structure === The mound, initially covered in bamboo, had been heavily modified by later activities. Excavations revealed that the mound, originally believed to be keyhole-shaped, is {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|lt=scallop-shaped}}. It consists of three tiers and measures: * Total length: 51 meters * Diameter of the rear section: 38.4 meters * Length of the frontal section: 12.5 meters * Width of the frontal section: Approximately 31 meters * Height of the rear section: Over 6.5 meters The mound was covered with fukiishi (葺石), sourced locally within 500 meters. The tiers were encircled with cylindrical haniwa, along with jar- and lid-shaped haniwa. Seven lid-shaped haniwa were uncovered across three excavations. === built === Based on these findings, archaeologist {{ill|Hiroshi Tsude|ja|都出比呂志}}[[ja:都出比呂志]] estimates the mound's construction to around the late 4th century to early 5th century. == other_designation == === National other_designation === * Toriimae Kofun (part of the Otokuni Kofun Group) * Designated on March 1, 2016, along with other mounds in the Otokuni Kofun Group, including {{ill|Tenno-no-Mori Kofun|ja|天皇の杜古墳|1=Tenno-no-Mori Kofun}}[[ja:天皇の杜古墳]], {{ill|Egayama Kofun|ja|恵解山古墳|1=Igenoyama Kofun}}[[ja:恵解山古墳]], and {{ill|Terado Otsuka Kofun|ja|寺戸大塚古墳|1=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun}}[[ja:寺戸大塚古墳]].2016 Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31 == References == Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{日本の古墳 |名称=鳥居前古墳 |所属=乙訓古墳群 |image={{画像募集中|cat=乙訓郡}} |location=京都府乙訓郡大山崎町円明寺 |type=帆立貝形古墳 |area=墳丘長51メートル |築造年代= 4世紀末頃から5世紀初頭 |埋葬施設=竪穴式石室 |被葬者=不明 |burial goods=画文帯神獣鏡片・勾玉・巴形銅器 |史跡指定=国の[[史跡]]「鳥居前古墳」
（「乙訓古墳群」に包含）{{Cite web|和書|author=大山崎町生涯学習課文化芸術係|url=https://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000061976.html|title=鳥居前古墳の概要リーフレットはこちらからご覧になれます。|work=大山崎町|date=2019-06-21|accessdate=2022-02-11}} |位置={{coord|34|54|42.5|N|135|40|39.2|E|region:JP-26|display=inline,title}} |地図2={{location map|Japan Kyoto|lat_deg=34|lat_min=54|lat_sec=42.5|lon_deg=135|lon_min=40|lon_sec=39.2|label=鳥居前古墳|mark=帆立貝形古墳-北北東.svg|marksize=28|border=none|caption=京都府内の位置|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|54|42.5|N|135|40|39.2|E}}}} |特記事項= }} {{nihongo|'''Torii-mae Kofun'''|[[:ja:鳥居前古墳|鳥居前古墳]][[ja:鳥居前古墳]]}} is a {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} (帆立貝形古墳) located in {{ill|Ōyamazaki Town|ja|大山崎町}}[[ja:大山崎町]], Otokuni District, Kyoto Prefecture. It is designation1 as a National other_designation as part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Group|ja|乙訓古墳群}}[[ja:乙訓古墳群]]. The mound was discovered in a 1967 survey and has since undergone three archaeological investigations by Kyoto Prefecture and Osaka University. == Overview == Toriimae Kofun is situated on the northern slopes of Mount Tennozan, known for the famous Battle of Yamazaki, at an elevation of 97 meters. The Kofun was identified during a distribution survey conducted by the Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education in 1967. Excavations of the rear circular section and part of the mound were conducted in 1969. Initially thought to be a [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]] (前方後円墳), subsequent investigations revised the designation to a {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}. === burial and Grave Goods === The rear circular section contains a single {{ill|Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} (竪穴式石室), oriented diagonally to the main axis of the mound. Measurements are as follows: * Burial chamber: 9.0 × 4.4 meters * Stone chamber: Length 6.5 meters, width 1.2 meters (west end), 0.77 meters (east end) The chamber's upper stones and ceiling stones were already missing. A clay bed within the chamber likely housed a split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin (割竹形木棺) of 5.3 meters in length. The coffin bed contained: * 2 [[magatama]] * 23 {{ill|Kudatama|ja|管玉|lt=cylindrical beads}} (管玉) * 8 iron swords * 21 daggers * 9 short swords * 2 comma-shaped copper objects (巴形銅器) Additional grave goods, including swords, arrows, armor fragments, and fragments of a deity-beast mirror (画文帯神獣鏡), were found in disturbed soil within the chamber. === Mound Structure === The mound, initially covered in bamboo, had been heavily modified by later activities. Excavations revealed that the mound, originally believed to be keyhole-shaped, is {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|lt=scallop-shaped}}. It consists of three tiers and measures: * Total length: 51 meters * Diameter of the rear section: 38.4 meters * Length of the frontal section: 12.5 meters * Width of the frontal section: Approximately 31 meters * Height of the rear section: Over 6.5 meters The mound was covered with fukiishi (葺石), sourced locally within 500 meters. The tiers were encircled with cylindrical haniwa, along with jar- and lid-shaped haniwa. Seven lid-shaped haniwa were uncovered across three excavations. === built === Based on these findings, archaeologist {{ill|Hiroshi Tsude|ja|都出比呂志}}[[ja:都出比呂志]] estimates the mound's construction to around the late 4th century to early 5th century. == other_designation == === National other_designation === * Toriimae Kofun (part of the Otokuni Kofun Group) * Designated on March 1, 2016, along with other mounds in the Otokuni Kofun Group, including {{ill|Tenno-no-Mori Kofun|ja|天皇の杜古墳|1=Tenno-no-Mori Kofun}}[[ja:天皇の杜古墳]], {{ill|Egayama Kofun|ja|恵解山古墳|1=Igenoyama Kofun}}[[ja:恵解山古墳]], and {{ill|Terado Otsuka Kofun|ja|寺戸大塚古墳|1=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun}}[[ja:寺戸大塚古墳]].2016 Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31 == References == But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{日本の古墳 |名称=鳥居前古墳 |所属=乙訓古墳群 |image={{画像募集中|cat=乙訓郡}} |location=京都府乙訓郡大山崎町円明寺 |type=帆立貝形古墳 |area=墳丘長51メートル |築造年代= 4世紀末頃から5世紀初頭 |埋葬施設=竪穴式石室 |被葬者=不明 |burial goods=画文帯神獣鏡片・勾玉・巴形銅器 |史跡指定=国の[[史跡]]「鳥居前古墳」
（「乙訓古墳群」に包含）{{Cite web|和書|author=大山崎町生涯学習課文化芸術係|url=https://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000061976.html|title=鳥居前古墳の概要リーフレットはこちらからご覧になれます。|work=大山崎町|date=2019-06-21|accessdate=2022-02-11}} |位置={{coord|34|54|42.5|N|135|40|39.2|E|region:JP-26|display=inline,title}} |地図2={{location map|Japan Kyoto|lat_deg=34|lat_min=54|lat_sec=42.5|lon_deg=135|lon_min=40|lon_sec=39.2|label=鳥居前古墳|mark=帆立貝形古墳-北北東.svg|marksize=28|border=none|caption=京都府内の位置|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|54|42.5|N|135|40|39.2|E}}}} |特記事項= }} {{nihongo|'''Torii-mae Kofun'''|[[:ja:鳥居前古墳|鳥居前古墳]][[ja:鳥居前古墳]]}} is a {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} (帆立貝形古墳) located in {{ill|Ōyamazaki Town|ja|大山崎町}}[[ja:大山崎町]], Otokuni District, Kyoto Prefecture. It is designation1 as a National other_designation as part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Group|ja|乙訓古墳群}}[[ja:乙訓古墳群]]. The mound was discovered in a 1967 survey and has since undergone three archaeological investigations by Kyoto Prefecture and Osaka University. == Overview == Toriimae Kofun is situated on the northern slopes of Mount Tennozan, known for the famous Battle of Yamazaki, at an elevation of 97 meters. The Kofun was identified during a distribution survey conducted by the Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education in 1967. Excavations of the rear circular section and part of the mound were conducted in 1969. Initially thought to be a [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]] (前方後円墳), subsequent investigations revised the designation to a {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}. === burial and burial goods === The rear circular section contains a single {{ill|Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} (竪穴式石室), oriented diagonally to the main axis of the mound. Measurements are as follows: * Burial chamber: 9.0 × 4.4 meters * Stone chamber: Length 6.5 meters, width 1.2 meters (west end), 0.77 meters (east end) The chamber's upper stones and ceiling stones were already missing. A clay bed within the chamber likely housed a split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin (割竹形木棺) of 5.3 meters in length. The coffin bed contained: * 2 [[magatama]] * 23 {{ill|Kudatama|ja|管玉|lt=cylindrical beads}} (管玉) * 8 iron swords * 21 daggers * 9 short swords * 2 comma-shaped copper objects (巴形銅器) Additional grave goods, including swords, arrows, armor fragments, and fragments of a deity-beast mirror (画文帯神獣鏡), were found in disturbed soil within the chamber. === Mound Structure === The mound, initially covered in bamboo, had been heavily modified by later activities. Excavations revealed that the mound, originally believed to be keyhole-shaped, is {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|lt=scallop-shaped}}. It consists of three tiers and measures: * Total length: 51 meters * Diameter of the rear section: 38.4 meters * Length of the frontal section: 12.5 meters * Width of the frontal section: Approximately 31 meters * Height of the rear section: Over 6.5 meters The mound was covered with fukiishi (葺石), sourced locally within 500 meters. The tiers were encircled with cylindrical haniwa, along with jar- and lid-shaped haniwa. Seven lid-shaped haniwa were uncovered across three excavations. === built === Based on these findings, archaeologist {{ill|Hiroshi Tsude|ja|都出比呂志}}[[ja:都出比呂志]] estimates the mound's construction to around the late 4th century to early 5th century. == other_designation == === National other_designation === * Toriimae Kofun (part of the Otokuni Kofun Group) * Designated on March 1, 2016, along with other mounds in the Otokuni Kofun Group, including {{ill|Tenno-no-Mori Kofun|ja|天皇の杜古墳|1=Tenno-no-Mori Kofun}}[[ja:天皇の杜古墳]], {{ill|Egayama Kofun|ja|恵解山古墳|1=Igenoyama Kofun}}[[ja:恵解山古墳]], and {{ill|Terado Otsuka Kofun|ja|寺戸大塚古墳|1=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun}}[[ja:寺戸大塚古墳]].2016 Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 31 == References ==
